  3. 我願意在哪裡投入資本來換取運行節省？

然後對照表格逐行篩選，就能快速縮小選型範圍。

Original Text

6.6 Reverse Osmosis (RO) for WFI

RO employs a semi-permeable membrane and a relatively high pressure differential to force water through the membrane to achieve chemical, microbial, and endotoxin reduction, critical in USP WFI applications. The feed water is converted into two streams, permeate and reject. The permeate water flows through the membrane and is produced cold and as such does not have the temperature protection for microbial growth afforded by the alternate distillation processes.

6.6.1 Application

RO systems are used as USP WFI pretreatment for distillation processes, or as final treatment for USP Purified water systems. RO is also an accepted means of producing WFI, and may provide a low capital and operational cost alternative to distillation.

Membranes that are hot water sanitizable at 80°C are now available for pretreatment and final treatment, thus eliminating the need for chemical sanitization and simplifying the validation process.

Membranes, which may allow for continuous operation at 80°C, are under development. Failure of a membrane or seal will result in permeate contamination. These problems may be controlled by:

  • Pretreatment of the feed water
  • Appropriate membrane material selection
  • Latest technology membrane design
  • Integrity challenges
  • Periodic sanitization
  • Monitoring of microbial levels, conductivity, total organic carbon and differential pressures

6.6.2 Description

Semi-permeable RO membranes are produced commercially in spiral wound and hollow fiber configurations. Membranes are available in four basic materials: cellulose acetate, polyamide, thin film composite, and polysulfone.

Bacteria and endotoxin removal, required for WFI applications, can be performed at ambient temperatures. This significantly reduces utility costs compared to alternative elevated temperature processes (distillation). By operating at ambient temperatures, distribution piping may not require insulation and may not need to be constructed of stainless steel.

For WFI applications, opportunities exist for enhanced control of the single pass unit, by utilizing multi-pass-product-staged or other combination designs.

Commentary / 教學解析

RO 製備 WFI 解析

RO 作為 WFI 生產手段

RO（Reverse Osmosis，逆滲透）利用半透膜在高壓下將水與雜質分離：

  • Permeate（透過液） — 通過膜的純水，即產品水
  • Reject（濃縮液） — 被膜阻擋的雜質濃縮水，排放

RO 最大特點：產出常溫水。這既是優勢（低能耗），也是挑戰（沒有溫度抑菌保護）。

RO-WFI 的核心挑戰：微生物控制

蒸餾系統在 80-100°C 下運行，天然抑制微生物。RO 在常溫下運行，必須靠其他手段控制微生物：

  • 80°C 熱水消毒膜（Hot water sanitizable membrane）
  • 定期化學消毒/清洗
  • 完整性測試（Integrity challenge）
  • 連續監測電導度、TOC、微生物與壓差

RO 膜材質選擇

膜材質特點
Cellulose Acetate（醋酸纖維素）耐氯，但 pH 範圍窄，易水解
Polyamide（聚醯胺）高脫鹽率，不耐氯
Thin Film Composite（薄膜複合）性能與聚醯胺相似，最常用
Polysulfone（聚碸）耐熱性較佳，適合 UF 應用

比喻：RO vs. 蒸餾 = 冷水洗手 vs. 熱水洗手

蒸餾就像用熱水洗手 — 高溫本身就能殺菌。RO 就像用冷水加肥皂（膜）洗手 — 需要額外的清潔劑（消毒程序）來確保衛生，但更省能源。

Original Text

6.6.3 Pretreatment Requirements

RO, as the final processing step, may require pretreatment using ion exchange, deionization, RO, and/or ultrafiltration to improve operability and quality attributes.

Pretreatment requirements normally include gross particle filtration, scale prevention, and chlorine removal. Carbon dioxide and ammonia gas are not removed by the RO process, and may be removed by degasification, caustic addition, ion exchange, or electrodeionization, prior to the final RO process step.

Due to the stringent microbial and endotoxin control required, the pretreatment prior to the RO should incorporate additional provisions for control and monitoring of microorganisms. Disinfectants, such as chlorine or chloramine, should be maintained at appropriate levels throughout the pretreatment chain. Stagnant water should be avoided by design, or by the inclusion of recirculation systems with in-line UV sterilizers.

Regular and appropriate sanitization and cleaning of all unit operations subsequent to the disinfectant removal should be scheduled.

6.6.4 Economics

Operating costs of RO systems are associated mainly with replacement membranes, water concentrate discharge, electrical power, cleaning and sanitizing chemicals, replacement filters, and pretreatment cost.

6.6.5 Construction Materials

Construction material selection for RO systems are driven by structural integrity (high pressure), chemical compatibility, and the need to control micro-organism growth.

The low operating temperature of the RO system allows the use of non-metallic construction materials. Sanitary piping and valves are generally optional features. For the final purification step, it may be very cost effective to utilize mill finish 304 stainless steel for the feed and concentrate waste piping, maintaining 316L stainless steel or PVDF and sanitary design for the product piping only.

6.6.6-7 Surface Finish & Instrumentation

MP and EP processes are not applicable to non-metallic systems. RO control systems usually use local control and indication and do not typically require PLCs as a standard feature. The typically monitored parameters are feed pH, feed conductivity, and product quality (TOC and conductivity), using calibratable, NIST traceable instruments.

Commentary / 教學解析

RO 前處理與經濟性

RO-WFI 前處理策略

RO 作為 WFI 最終步驟時，前處理鏈需要比一般 PW 系統更嚴格：

  • 粗過濾 — 保護膜不受顆粒損壞
  • 防垢處理 — 防止鈣鎂結垢
  • 除氯 — 保護聚醯胺/TFC 膜
  • 微生物控制 — 維持消毒劑（氯/氯胺）直到除氯點
  • 死水預防 — 循環系統 + UV 殺菌

RO 的成本優勢與隱藏成本

顯而易見的優勢：

  • 不需蒸汽 — 僅用電力驅動高壓泵浦
  • 常溫運行 — 管線不需保溫，材質選擇更靈活

容易被忽視的成本：

  • 膜的更換費用（定期消耗品）
  • 清洗與消毒化學品
  • 濃縮水排放（水資源浪費）
  • 前處理系統的維護

材質選擇的 GEP 原則

RO 系統的一個重大成本優勢：因為常溫運行，可以使用非金屬材料（如 PVDF、PVC）。只有產品水管線需要 316L SS 或 PVDF 衛生設計。進水和廢水管線甚至可以用普通 304 SS 就行。

這與蒸餾系統幾乎全面使用 316L SS 形成鮮明對比，是 RO 資本成本較低的重要原因之一。

Original Text

6.6.8 RO Advantages and Disadvantages

Multi-pass can, in most cases, produce water quality consistent with the minimum requirements of USP WFI. In cases where the feed water quality is such that this is not possible, the use of some type of deionization as a pretreatment may be required.

Advantages:

  1. RO systems designed for production of USP WFI may provide for significantly reduced capital costs when compared with distillation processes, while maintaining the appropriate USP WFI quality.
  2. The utility requirements are significantly lower for RO systems (electricity for pump horsepower) than for distillation, resulting in lower operating costs, which may be a very significant factor over the lifetime of the system.

Disadvantages:

  1. Membrane fouling and integrity:
    • Bacteria grow-through
    • Seal leakage or by-passing
    • Seal failure or damage caused by chemical attack
    • Membrane damage during installation
    • Membrane damage due to chemical or high temperature attack
  2. Membrane material sensitivity to bacteria and sanitizing agents
  3. Inherent sanitization limitations:
    • Periodic chemical or hot water sanitization may be required
    • Periodic chemical cleaning may be required
  4. Pretreatment cost may be high

RO systems provide a method for consistently producing ambient water in accordance with the USP WFI requirements. This not only reduces utility requirements, but also may reduce installation costs, since thermal insulation may not be required for storage and distribution.

Commentary / 教學解析

RO 優缺點分析

RO-WFI 優劣勢總覽

面向優勢劣勢
資本成本顯著低於蒸餾前處理可能增加成本
運行成本僅需電力，遠低於蒸餾膜更換、清洗為持續費用
安裝成本不需保溫，管材選擇靈活—
微生物控制—無溫度保護，需額外措施
膜完整性—密封洩漏、細菌穿透等風險
驗證複雜度—需驗證消毒有效性、膜完整性

最大風險：Bacteria Grow-Through

「細菌穿透」是 RO-WFI 系統最受關注的風險。與蒸餾的相變分離（100°C 蒸發）不同，RO 膜在常溫下運行，長期使用後膜的微觀結構可能讓細菌逐漸穿透。這要求：

  • 定期完整性測試
  • 嚴格的消毒週期
  • 持續監測產品水微生物

思考練習：選 RO 還是蒸餾？

考慮以下場景，你會推薦 RO-WFI 還是蒸餾-WFI？

  1. 新建小型生物製劑工廠，預算有限，東南亞廠區，電費便宜
  2. 歐洲大型充填工廠（2017 年以前），需同時供應 WFI 和 Clean Steam
  3. 已有完善冷卻水和高壓蒸汽系統的老廠擴建

提示：考慮法規、公用設施條件、Clean Steam 需求、以及生命週期成本。

Original Text

6.7 USP — Water for Injection Systems Comparison

Table 6-4: WFI Systems Comparison

Ratings: L = Low, M = Medium, H = High

Unit Operation (1,2)2-Pass ROSEMEVC
Capital CostMMHH
Chemical ConsumptionLN/AN/AN/A
Energy ConsumptionMHHH
Water ConsumptionM (3)HMM
Outside Service CostsLLLL
Operational MaintenanceLLLL

Notes:
(1) All indicators are relative to each other within the specific category
(2) Optimum design and operating conditions are assumed
(3) Total water consumption is dependent on pretreatment selected
(4) RO may not meet USP TOC levels if feed water TOC is high (>3 ppm)

Commentary / 教學解析

WFI 系統總比較

Table 6-4 重點解讀

  • 資本成本：ME 和 VC 最高（H），RO 和 SE 中等（M）
  • 能源消耗：三種蒸餾都是高（H），RO 只有中等（M）— 這是 RO 的最大優勢
  • 水消耗：SE 最高（H），其餘都是中等（M）
  • 化學品消耗：只有 RO 有（用於清洗/消毒），蒸餾不需要
  • 維護和外部服務：四者差異不大，都是 Low

決策矩陣思維

選擇 WFI 生產方式時，需要權衡以下因素：

決策因素傾向 RO傾向蒸餾
能源成本電價便宜蒸汽便宜或已有鍋爐
資本預算有限充裕
法規環境USP / JP / EP 2017+任何法規均接受
Clean Steam 需求不需要需要（選 ME）
微生物風險容忍度有完善監測能力偏保守/簡化驗證
出水溫度常溫可接受需要 80°C+ 熱循環

注意事項 (4)：TOC 風險

如果進水 TOC > 3 ppm，RO 可能無法滿足 USP WFI 的 TOC 規格（500 ppb）。這種情況下需要額外的 TOC 去除前處理，或改用蒸餾。進水 TOC 評估是 RO-WFI 可行性評估的第一步。

Chapter Summary / 章節總結

  • WFI 是最高等級製藥用水，用於注射劑、部分眼科/吸入劑產品及注射級 API。內毒素控制是核心要求。
  • 四種最終處理技術：SE 蒸餾（小量/簡單）、ME 蒸餾（中大量/可兼產 Clean Steam）、VC 蒸餾（大量/電力驅動/可產常溫水）、RO（低成本替代方案）。
  • 蒸餾的金標準地位：相變分離（100°C）提供天然的微生物屏障，內毒素去除 ≥ 3 log。所有藥典均接受。
  • RO 的崛起：USP 允許、JP 允許、EP 於 2017 年也接受。能耗與資本成本低，但需嚴格的微生物控制與膜完整性管理。
  • 前處理是系統成敗的關鍵：ME 需要最高品質的進水（TDS < 10, SiO2 < 1），所有蒸餾器都需要除氯，RO 需要防垢和微生物控制。
  • GEP 原則貫穿全章：不要過度指定表面處理（MP 佔 25-30% 成本），材質選擇應基於功能需求而非盲目追求高規格。
  • 選型決策：沒有單一「最佳」方案。需綜合考量法規環境、公用設施條件、Clean Steam 需求、微生物風險管理能力與生命週期成本。

ISPE Baseline® Guide Volume 4: Water and Steam Systems

Chapter 6: Final Treatment Options — Water for Injection (WFI) | p75-p87

SterileGMP Knowledge Hub — Bilingual Educational Reference

7

Chapter 7: Pharmaceutical Steam

製藥蒸汽 | p89-p109

ISPE Baseline® Guide Volume 4: Water and Steam Systems | p89-p98

Learning Objectives | 學習目標

  • Understand the regulatory landscape (or lack thereof) for pharmaceutical steam | 了解製藥蒸汽的法規環境（及其空白之處）
  • Distinguish between steam Quality (saturation/dryness) and Purity (chemical/endotoxin content) | 區分蒸汽「品質」（飽和度/乾度）與「純度」（化學/內毒素含量）
  • Classify Utility Steam vs. Clean Steam and their appropriate applications | 分類廠用蒸汽與潔淨蒸汽及其適用場景
  • Evaluate industry practices for sterilizing steam and humidification | 評估業界滅菌蒸汽與加濕蒸汽的慣例
  • Apply the Pharmaceutical Steam System Planning methodology | 運用製藥蒸汽系統規劃方法論
  • Navigate the Pharmaceutical Steam Purity Decision Tree for correct steam grade selection | 使用蒸汽純度決策樹選擇正確的蒸汽等級
Original Text | 原文

7.1 Introduction

This chapter aims to simplify and standardize the process of selection, programming, and design of pharmaceutical steam systems. Guidelines, information, and options are provided, along with advantages and disadvantages, based on the best and most cost effective of current and proven practices and technologies.

The absence of regulations governing the use of steam in pharmaceutical processes has resulted in the proliferation of differing practices and interpretations. Most interpretations are made on the side of conservatism. Unfortunately, in addition to increasing cost without an associated increase in benefits, excessive conservatism can result in system complexity, and possibly reduced reliability.

One example is the use of clean steam (non-utility boiler produced steam) where a form of utility steam (utility boiler produced steam) would be adequate to maintain product quality. The installation of a clean steam generator when a simple steam reducing station would suffice results in added equipment and the associated impact on cost, complexity, and reliability.

In some instances, interpretations are based on inaccurate assumptions of what is important or critical. An example is the over specifying of pretreatment or using WFI as feed to solve the perceived problem.

Commentary | 教學解說

導讀：為何蒸汽是「灰色地帶」？

核心訊息

製藥蒸汽是少數「缺乏明確法規」的關鍵公用系統之一。FDA 和 USP 對水系統有詳盡規範，但對蒸汽卻幾乎沒有最低標準。這導致業界普遍「寧可做過頭」，反而造成：

  • 成本增加 — 不必要的潔淨蒸汽產生器
  • 系統複雜化 — 更多設備 = 更多故障點
  • 可靠性降低 — 過度設計反而增加維護負擔

比喻：用大砲打蚊子

想像你的工廠只需要「乾淨的自來水」就能洗手，但因為不確定法規要求，你決定用「注射用水（WFI）」來洗。水質當然合格，但成本高出10倍，而且 WFI 系統更難維護。蒸汽也一樣 — 很多工廠在只需要 Utility Steam 的地方用了 Clean Steam。

實務重點

本章的核心價值：提供一套基於風險的決策框架，讓你不再「猜測」該用什麼等級的蒸汽，而是根據產品接觸風險、製程需求來做出合理選擇。

Original Text | 原文

7.2 cGMP Issues

The user has the ultimate responsibility for system design and performance, and for ensuring that the proper type of steam is used for a given process.

There is no FDA or USP minimum standard for clean steam. However, cGMPs for large volume parenterals (LVPs) issued in 1976 indicated that feed water for boilers supplying steam that contact components, drug products, and drug product contact surfaces shall not contain volatile additives such as amines or hydrazines.

Few regulations govern the design and construction of clean steam generators. There are also no regulations governing materials of construction, type or level of instrumentation, surface finishes, or operating temperatures.

Among US Government publications, the FDA's Code of Federal Regulations (CFR) provides culinary steam recommendations and stipulations related to heat exchanger and tank air vents design and construction. The Culinary steam recommendations apply to food applications only.

US Public Health Service/Dairy Industry Committee, 3A Sanitary Standards, Number 609-02, adds additional limitations to Culinary steam feed water additives for food applications. It should be noted that boiler feed water additives permitted in food for human consumption may not be acceptable in drinking water or orally ingested drug products.

Commentary | 教學解說

法規環境解析

關鍵發現：法規近乎空白

這是本章最重要的觀念之一：Clean Steam 幾乎沒有法規標準。具體來說：

  • FDA 沒有 Clean Steam 最低標準
  • USP 沒有 Clean Steam 專論（monograph）
  • 沒有規範建材、表面粗糙度、儀控等級
  • 唯一明確的要求：1976年 LVP cGMP — 禁止胺類/聯氨類揮發性添加劑

誰負責？— 使用者自負

「The user has the ultimate responsibility」— 這句話在 GMP 世界裡份量很重。意思是：

  • 法規不會告訴你「用哪種蒸汽」
  • 你必須自己評估風險、選擇合適等級
  • 稽核時，你要能說明你的選擇邏輯（rationale）

這就是為什麼後面的 Decision Tree（7.6節）如此重要。

比喻：Culinary Steam 的陷阱

Culinary Steam 是「食品級蒸汽」— 聽起來很安全？但注意：食品容許的鍋爐添加劑（如某些胺類），在藥品製造中可能完全不可接受。這就像食品級的防腐劑不能直接加入注射液一樣。製藥標準永遠高於食品標準。

Original Text | 原文

7.2.1 Steam Attributes

7.2.1.1 Quality

The term "Quality" when referring to steam indicates the level of steam saturation. There are no FDA or USP regulations relating to minimum "steam quality" or the level of non-condensable gasses present in pharmaceutical steam.

European regulators have defined specific criteria for pharmaceutical steam used for equipment sterilization. (European Standard EN 285 - Steam Sterilizers - reference section 13.3) These cover acceptable levels of saturation or dryness, the level of superheat, and the volume of non-condensable gases present.

7.2.1.2 Purity

Purity requirements for steam used in pharmaceutical manufacturing and product development are driven by the product characteristics, manufacturing process, and the intended use of the product. The product manufacturer is responsible for ensuring that steam used to process the product is appropriate.

Though steam purity requirements are product specific, it may be impractical to reliably produce special steam for each situation. Manufacturing operations typically generate and distribute only one or two steam purity grades, commonly grouped.

Commentary | 教學解說

Quality vs. Purity — 最容易混淆的概念

兩個完全不同的概念

屬性 Quality（品質） Purity（純度）
定義 蒸汽的飽和程度（乾度） 蒸汽中化學物質/內毒素含量
關注重點 含水量、過熱度、不凝結氣體 胺類、雜質、微生物副產物
影響 滅菌效率（濕包、冷點） 產品污染風險
法規 歐盟 EN 285 有明確標準 FDA/USP 無最低標準

比喻：瓶裝水 vs. 自來水

Quality 就像水的「溫度和壓力」— 你需要的是熱水，但水龍頭流出來的是溫的（不夠飽和）或太燙（過熱），都不好用。

Purity 就像水裡「含什麼成分」— 即使溫度壓力都對，但水裡有鐵鏽或化學藥劑，就不能用在產品上。

兩者獨立但都重要：高 Quality 低 Purity 的蒸汽（乾但含雜質）不適合產品接觸；低 Quality 高 Purity 的蒸汽（濕但乾淨）滅菌效率差。

EN 285 — 歐盟的領先標準

美國 FDA/USP 沒有蒸汽品質標準，但歐盟 EN 285 規定了：

  • 乾度（Dryness）：≥ 0.95
  • 過熱度（Superheat）：≤ 25°C
  • 不凝結氣體（NCG）：≤ 3.5%（體積）

實務上，即使在美國工廠，EN 285 常被作為內部蒸汽品質標準使用。

Original Text | 原文

7.3 Types of Steam

Pharmaceutical steam is classified into two (2) types based on their respective sources:

  1. Utility-Boiler produced steam, hereafter called Utility Steam.
  2. Non-Utility Boiler produced steam, hereafter called Clean Steam.

7.3.1 Utility Steam

Utility steam is characterized with usually having:

  • Chemical additives to control scale and corrosion
  • Relatively high pressure with the potential of generating superheat during expansion
  • Relatively high pH

Chemical additives: Utility steam is produced, in most cases, using conventional fire-tube steam boilers, normally of steel construction. Such boilers are almost always provided with systems that inject additives in the feed water to protect the boiler and steam distribution piping from scale and corrosion. Some of these scale and corrosion inhibitors may, and often do, include amines and other substances that may not be acceptable in steam being used in pharmaceutical processes.

Utility steam can be filtered to remove particulate matter, but filtration does not remove dissolved substances and volatiles such as amines.

Superheat: Superheated steam is produced in water tube boilers by reheating the steam or by generating the steam at a higher pressure in a fire tube boiler and then reducing the pressure through a regulating valve. When the pressure is reduced, the energy in the higher temperature steam is dissipated to generate steam at the lower pressure and produce superheated steam above the corresponding saturation temperature.

pH control: In order to protect carbon steel from corrosion by the steam, it is necessary to use additives to raise the pH to between 9.5 - 10.5.

Commentary | 教學解說

兩種蒸汽的比較

分類邏輯：看「來源」而非「品質」

蒸汽分類很簡單 — 只看它從哪裡來：

  • Utility Steam = 從工廠公用鍋爐來（有添加劑）
  • Clean Steam = 從專用產生器來（無添加劑）

這不是「好壞」之分，而是「用途」之分。很多場景下 Utility Steam 完全合格。

Utility Steam 的三大特徵

  1. 化學添加劑 — 防垢防蝕，但含胺類/聯氨類
  2. 過熱（Superheat） — 高壓減壓後產生，對滅菌不利
  3. 高 pH — 9.5-10.5，保護碳鋼管路

過濾不等於純化

這是非常重要的觀念：Utility Steam 可以過濾去除顆粒物（particulate），但無法去除溶解物和揮發物（如胺類）。就像水的活性碳濾芯能擋住沙子，但擋不住溶解的氯。

所以如果應用需要「無添加劑」的蒸汽，過濾 Utility Steam 是不夠的 — 必須使用 Clean Steam。

過熱蒸汽 — 為何滅菌時是壞事？

過熱蒸汽（Superheated Steam）溫度高於飽和溫度，行為更像「熱空氣」而非蒸汽。滅菌需要的是飽和蒸汽的潛熱（latent heat）釋放 — 蒸汽凝結在物體表面時放出大量熱能。過熱蒸汽不凝結，傳熱效率大幅下降。

就像用吹風機烘乾和用蒸氣熨斗燙衣服的差別 — 蒸氣熨斗效果更好，因為凝結釋放的熱量更集中。

Original Text | 原文

7.3.2 Clean Steam (CS)

Pharmaceutical clean steam is generated from treated water free of volatile additives, such as amines or hydrazines, and is used for thermal disinfection or sterilization processes. It is considered especially important to preclude such contamination from injectable drug products.

Clean steam is characterized as having:

  • No additives
  • No generated superheat except when the generated pressure is significantly higher than the use pressure of the steam
  • Relatively low pH

There are many terms used in the pharmaceutical industry to describe Clean Steam. These include Clean Steam, Pure Steam, Pyrogen Free Steam, WFI Steam, and USP Purified Water steam. There is no standard or accepted definition for any of these terms. However, the most commonly used terms are "Pure Steam" and "Clean Steam". In this Guide, the term "Clean Steam" is used in lieu of all others.

The condensate of Clean Steam has no buffer, and may have a relatively low pH compared to that of utility steam.

Commentary | 教學解說

Clean Steam 深入解析

Clean Steam 三大特徵

特徵 Utility Steam Clean Steam
添加劑 有（防垢/防蝕劑） 無
過熱 常見（減壓產生） 極少（除非壓差很大）
pH 高（9.5-10.5） 低（無緩衝劑）
管路材質 碳鋼 316L 不鏽鋼
應用 加熱、HVAC、非關鍵 滅菌、產品接觸

名稱混亂 — 業界痛點

Clean Steam、Pure Steam、Pyrogen Free Steam、WFI Steam... 同一件事至少有5種叫法，而且沒有任何一個有標準定義。這在跨國稽核或設備採購時經常造成誤解。

ISPE 的建議：統一使用 "Clean Steam" 一詞。在你的 SOP 和 URS 中也應該明確定義你用的術語。

低 pH 的隱藏風險

Clean Steam 凝結水沒有緩衝劑，pH 可能偏低（接近純水的 ~5.5-6.5）。這代表：

  • 管路必須用耐蝕材質（316L 不鏽鋼）
  • 碳鋼管路會被快速腐蝕
  • 這也是 Clean Steam 系統成本較高的原因之一
Original Text | 原文

7.4 Background and Industry Practices

7.4.1 Purity of Sterilizing Steam

When steam or the resulting condensed water comes in direct or indirect contact with the drug product, the purity should be equivalent to the water purity acceptable for final rinsing of the drug contact surfaces.

Note: A continuous supply of Dry Saturated Steam at the point of use is considered necessary for efficient steam sterilization. Water carried by the steam in suspension may cause damp loads and superheated steam is considerably less effective than saturated steam when used for sterilization. Non-condensable gases if contained in the steam may prevent the attainment of sterilization conditions in parts of the sterilizer load.

7.4.2 Steam Used for Humidification

When steam is used for indirect humidification, such as injection into HVAC air streams prior to final air filtration, the steam does not need to be purer than the air that it is being mixed with.

However, when humidifying process areas, the potential level of impurities, including amines and hydrazines should be evaluated in order to ascertain the impact on the final drug product. This is particularly important in areas where open processing takes place, such as aseptic filling suites and formulation areas.

7.4.3 Common Practices

It is common practice to generate pharmaceutical steam from compendial waters and test the steam condensate for equivalency to the compendial standard. This practice ignores the ability of the pharmaceutical steam generator to remove impurities. This overprocessing is wasteful and unnecessary.

An exception is when the steam quantity is small and the cost and maintenance of a dedicated feed water pretreatment system exceeds the cost of using compendial water.

Commentary | 教學解說

業界實務 — 過度保守的代價

滅菌蒸汽純度原則

核心規則很簡單：蒸汽（或其凝結水）接觸產品時，純度應等同於該產品可接受的最終淋洗水純度。

例如：滅菌注射劑設備 → 凝結水應等同 WFI 品質。非無菌 API 中間體 → 可能 PW 品質就足夠。

滅菌蒸汽的三大敵人

  1. 含水（Wet Steam） → 造成「濕包（damp loads）」→ 滅菌失敗
  2. 過熱（Superheat） → 傳熱效率差 → 溫度到了但殺不死菌
  3. 不凝結氣體（NCG） → 形成氣囊（air pockets）→ 蒸汽無法到達死角

這三者影響的是蒸汽「Quality」而非「Purity」，但都會導致滅菌失敗。

加濕蒸汽：風險取決於「開放性」

想像一下：

  • 辦公室空調加濕 → Utility Steam 完全OK（蒸汽不接觸產品）
  • 一般潔淨室加濕 → 需要評估（蒸汽進入空氣，但有 HEPA 過濾）
  • 無菌充填室加濕 → 必須謹慎評估（開放製程，蒸汽可能接觸產品）

關鍵判斷：蒸汽中的雜質經稀釋後，是否會顯著影響產品品質？

常見浪費：用 WFI 產 Clean Steam

ISPE 明確指出這是「overprocessing, wasteful and unnecessary」。Clean Steam Generator 本身就是蒸餾器 — 它能去除雜質。用 WFI 作為進水等於「蒸餾兩次」，毫無必要。

唯一例外：蒸汽需求量極小時，專用前處理系統的成本可能超過直接使用 WFI。

Original Text | 原文

7.4.4 Industry and Baseline Practices in the Production of Steam

Table 7-1: Practices in the Production of Steam

Intended Use of Steam Method of Generation
Parenteral & Non-Parenteral dosage form: steam in direct contact with drug SCSG is both baseline and common industry practice
Critical step in API manufacture: steam in direct contact with API SCSG is both baseline and common industry practice
Non-critical step in API manufacture (impurities removable in subsequent step) SCSG commonly used; however, utility steam is acceptable baseline
Sterilization of USP water systems SCSG common; alternative: Utility Steam + hot USP water flushing & waste testing
Sterilization of fermentation vessels Baseline: utility steam
Process humidification (open processing, amines impact drug) SCSG is baseline when impurities have detrimental effect; utility steam if insignificant
Non-critical HVAC humidification SCSG commonly used but utility steam may be totally acceptable
Energy source for non-critical & cGMP heat exchangers SCSG common; baseline: utility steam + cGMP heat exchanger design

SCSG = Sanitary Clean Steam Generator

Commentary | 教學解說

Table 7-1 解讀：哪些地方可以省？

Baseline vs. Common Practice — 差異很大

這張表揭示了一個重要事實：業界「常見做法」往往超過「基準要求」。

  • Baseline = 合規所需的最低標準
  • Common Practice = 業界普遍做法（常因保守而過度）

可以省錢的三個場景

  1. API 非關鍵步驟 — Utility Steam 就夠（後續步驟會去除雜質）
  2. USP 水系統滅菌 — 可用 Utility Steam + 熱 USP 水沖洗 + 廢水測試
  3. 非關鍵 HVAC 加濕 — Utility Steam 完全可接受

這三個場景如果都用 SCSG，每年多花的錢可能數十萬美元。

思考練習

你的工廠有以下蒸汽使用點，哪些需要 Clean Steam？

  1. 無菌充填線 Autoclave 滅菌
  2. CIP 系統的 SIP 滅菌
  3. 辦公區空調加濕
  4. API 反應槽加熱（夾套間接加熱）
  5. 無菌灌裝室空調加濕

提示：思考蒸汽是否「直接或間接接觸產品」以及「後續是否有去除雜質的步驟」。

Original Text | 原文

7.5 System Planning

Pharmaceutical Steam System Planning, shown in Figure 7-1, is a graphic representation of the system boundaries, limitations, and restrictions. Initial system planning reveals primary boundaries that establish the cornerstone for design criteria. These system boundaries are:

  1. Steam Requirements
  2. System Design
  3. Use Point Criteria
  4. Distribution System requirements

The arrows encircling each boundary represent limitations that establish more specific operating strategies and ranges. To allow more flexibility in final planning and detailed design the designer should always indicate ranges of acceptability, rather than a specific value or position.

7.5.1 Steam Requirements

The planning process starts with the listing of all steam requirements and applications that include:

  • Company standards including QA/QC requirements and published SOPs
  • The categorization of use-point by:
    • Type of application (Humidification, critical or non-critical, API, and Dosage form applications)
    • Purity selection (based primarily on the application and the endotoxin and chemical purity criteria set for the product)
    • Steam quality (dryness, non-condensable limits, and maximum superheat)

7.5.2 System Design

Pharmaceutical steam is generated using different methods. The most appropriate method for each application must be selected. (See the Pharmaceutical Steam Purity Decision Tree, Section 7.6.)

The process continues with an evaluation of the steam system requirements (generation) that includes:

  • The types of generation systems available (if both pyrogen free clean steam and clean steam without endotoxin limits is required, the practicality and economy of producing only the higher grade should be raised)
  • The source of utility steam or electrical power
  • The type and number of systems required based on feedback from the "Distribution System" evaluation
  • The condensate sampling needs
  • Safety considerations
Commentary | 教學解說

系統規劃方法論

四大規劃邊界

ISPE 提出的系統規劃是一個迭代循環，包含四個相互關聯的邊界：

  1. Steam Requirements（蒸汽需求） — 列出所有使用點和應用
  2. System Design（系統設計） — 選擇產生方式
  3. Use Point Criteria（使用點標準） — 定義交付規格
  4. Distribution System（輸送系統） — 管路設計

這四個步驟不是線性的 — 每一步的結果都會回饋影響其他步驟，需要反覆迭代。

比喻：規劃蒸汽系統就像規劃自來水系統

想像你要為一棟新大樓設計供水系統：

  • 需求：哪些樓層需要飲用水？哪些只需要沖馬桶？
  • 設計：需要幾個水塔？要不要裝淨水器？
  • 使用點：每個水龍頭需要多少流量和壓力？
  • 輸送：管路怎麼走？要不要保溫？

蒸汽系統規劃邏輯完全相同，只是介質從水變成蒸汽。

設計智慧：用「範圍」而非「定值」

ISPE 特別強調：「indicate ranges of acceptability, rather than a specific value」。

例如：不要寫「壓力 3.0 bar」，要寫「壓力 2.5-3.5 bar」。範圍給設計留有餘裕，避免因微小偏差就「不合規」。

經濟性考量

7.5.2 提到一個務實建議：如果工廠同時需要「有內毒素限制的 Clean Steam」和「無內毒素限制的 Clean Steam」，評估是否乾脆只產高等級。有時一套高等級系統比兩套不同等級系統更經濟。

Original Text | 原文

7.5.3 Use Point Criteria

The third step defines the specific delivery requirement ranges for clean steam at the point of use including:

  • Utilization — determined from each overall system peak demand(s), average demand, and the relationships between peak demand time periods and their flow rates
  • Pressures and flow levels
  • Use periods and histogram analysis, if available
  • Quality
  • Purity

7.5.4 Distribution System

The fourth step includes the distribution system evaluation, which includes:

  • Condensate, non-condensable and moisture removal
  • Pipe size and Insulation requirements including:
    • Materials of construction, sanitary design requirements and surface finish
    • Physical location of each use point
    • Heat and temperature losses
    • Natural drainage
Note: Since the steam quality will decline, due to heat losses, with time, the efficiency of the insulation and the length of the distribution system, the quality at the use point will not be expected to reflect the generation quality level.

7.5.5 Re-evaluation of System Boundaries and Constraints

These sequential steps are repeated and re-evaluated as information in the design process iterates, and further criteria about the overall system boundaries are identified.

In operations with a requirement for only one grade of steam, the steam system is designed to meet the most stringent requirements of the most demanding product or process. With more than one purity grade of steam, products and processes are often categorized and fed by the most appropriate system.

Commentary | 教學解說

使用點與輸送系統

Use Point Criteria — 需求分析三要素

  • 需求量分析：尖峰需求 vs. 平均需求。蒸汽系統必須能應對尖峰，但不能過度設計。用 histogram（直方圖）分析用量分布最有效。
  • 壓力與流量：不同使用點需求不同（Autoclave 需要較高壓力，加濕需要較低壓力）。
  • 品質與純度：使用點的 Quality 和 Purity 要求。

輸送系統的隱藏挑戰

蒸汽管路不是「裝了就好」。管路越長：

  • 熱損失越大 → 蒸汽凝結 → Quality 下降
  • 凝結水積累 → 需要足夠的疏水閥（steam trap）
  • 不凝結氣體累積 → 需要排氣點
  • 管路傾斜 → 必須確保自然排水（natural drainage）

因此 ISPE 提醒：使用點的蒸汽品質不等於產生器出口的品質。

單等級 vs. 多等級策略

就像工廠用水策略：

  • 小工廠（使用點少）→ 只產一種最高等級的 Clean Steam，全廠共用。簡單、好管理。
  • 大工廠（使用點多、需求差異大）→ 分兩級：Clean Steam 給關鍵點，Utility Steam 給非關鍵點。省錢但系統更複雜。

決策關鍵：蒸汽消耗量和純度需求的差異程度。

設計思考題

你的工廠蒸汽管路從 Clean Steam Generator 到最遠的 Autoclave 有 150 公尺。你觀察到 Autoclave 偶爾出現「濕包」問題。可能的原因和解決方案是什麼？

提示：考慮管路長度、保溫效果、疏水閥配置、管路傾斜度。

Original Text | 原文

7.6 Pharmaceutical Steam Purity Decision Tree

Figure 7-2: Pharmaceutical Steam Purity Decision Tree

The Decision Tree provides a structured method for selecting the appropriate grade of steam based on the intended use and product contact risk. The following describes the decision logic:

START: What is the intended use of the steam?
▼
Q1: Does the steam or its condensate come in DIRECT contact with the drug product or drug product contact surfaces?
YES ▼
Use Clean Steam (SCSG)
Condensate purity equivalent to final rinse water standard
NO ▼
Q2: Is the steam used for sterilization of product contact equipment?
▼ (from Q2)
YES ▼
Use Clean Steam (SCSG)
Consider if post-SIP rinse with WFI is performed
NO ▼
Q3: Is the steam used for humidification of areas where open processing occurs?
▼ (from Q3)
YES ▼
Q3a: Do the impurities (amines, etc.) have a detrimental effect on the drug product?
YES → Use Clean Steam
NO → Utility Steam acceptable
NO ▼
Q4: Is the steam used as energy source (heat exchangers) or non-critical applications?
Utility Steam is the baseline approach
(use cGMP heat exchanger design where applicable)
Commentary | 教學解說

決策樹詳解 — 蒸汽選擇的核心工具

決策邏輯總結

Decision Tree 的邏輯可以簡化為一個核心問題：蒸汽（或其凝結水）是否接觸產品？

判斷條件 結論
直接接觸藥品/API Clean Steam（無爭議）
滅菌產品接觸設備 Clean Steam（凝結水等同最終淋洗水）
加濕開放製程區域 視雜質影響評估（可能是 Clean 或 Utility）
熱交換器/非關鍵應用 Utility Steam（基準做法）

最常見的誤區

  • 誤區1：「所有滅菌蒸汽都要 Clean Steam」→ 錯！發酵槽滅菌用 Utility Steam 就是 baseline。
  • 誤區2：「Clean Steam 一定要用 WFI 產」→ 錯！Steam Generator 本身就能去除雜質，處理水（Treated Water）通常就足夠。
  • 誤區3：「所有潔淨室加濕都要 Clean Steam」→ 錯！只有「開放製程」且雜質影響產品時才需要。

實務應用：快速判斷法

當有人問「這個點需要 Clean Steam 嗎？」，按以下順序回答：

  1. 蒸汽碰到產品嗎？→ YES = Clean Steam
  2. 蒸汽碰到「會碰產品的設備」嗎？→ YES = Clean Steam
  3. 蒸汽進入「開放製程的空氣」嗎？→ 要評估
  4. 以上都不是？→ Utility Steam 通常就夠

情境練習：Decision Tree 應用

判斷以下場景該用哪種蒸汽：

  1. SIP 滅菌無菌充填線的 holding tank
  2. API 合成反應中，蒸汽直接注入反應液
  3. 配方區 HVAC 加濕（封閉製程）
  4. WFI 儲槽的週期性 SIP
  5. 包裝區加熱用蒸汽（夾套加熱）

參考答案：1. Clean Steam, 2. Clean Steam, 3. 需評估（封閉製程可能 Utility 即可）, 4. Clean Steam 或 Utility+WFI沖洗, 5. Utility Steam

Section Summary | 本節摘要

  • 法規空白：FDA/USP 對 Clean Steam 沒有最低標準，使用者自負選擇責任。唯一明確禁令：LVP 相關蒸汽不得含揮發性添加劑（胺類/聯氨類）。
  • Quality vs. Purity：Quality = 飽和度/乾度（影響滅菌效率）；Purity = 化學/內毒素含量（影響產品安全）。兩者獨立且都重要。
  • 兩類蒸汽：Utility Steam（有添加劑、高 pH、可能過熱）vs. Clean Steam（無添加劑、低 pH、專用產生器）。分類依據是「來源」而非品質高低。
  • 業界過度保守：常見做法包括「用 WFI 產 Clean Steam」和「非關鍵應用也用 SCSG」，造成不必要的成本和複雜性。
  • 系統規劃四步驟：Steam Requirements → System Design → Use Point Criteria → Distribution System，迭代循環執行。
  • Decision Tree 核心邏輯：蒸汽是否接觸產品？→ 是否滅菌產品設備？→ 是否加濕開放製程？→ 依序判斷蒸汽等級。
  • 實務建議：用「範圍」而非「定值」規劃；小工廠可用單等級策略；輸送管路會降低蒸汽品質，需考慮保溫和疏水。
ISPE Baseline® Guide Volume 4: Water and Steam Systems | p99–p109

Learning Objectives

  1. Understand the operating principle of a Sanitary CS Generator (shell-and-tube evaporator, separator, feedback control) and distinguish it from a Simple CS Generator / 理解衛生型淨蒸汽發生器的運作原理（管殼式蒸發器、分離器、回饋控制），並與簡易型區分
  2. Size a Clean Steam system by evaluating peak demand, diversity, and generator capacity / 掌握淨蒸汽系統的 sizing 計算：尖峰需求、多樣性係數、發生器容量
  3. Explain steam condensate sampling for purity (endotoxin, conductivity) and quality (dryness fraction, NCGs) / 說明蒸汽凝結水取樣：純度（內毒素、電導度）與品質（乾燥度、不凝結氣體）
  4. Specify materials of construction, surface finish, and pretreatment requirements for CS generators / 指定淨蒸汽發生器的材質、表面處理與前處理要求
  5. Design a CS distribution system addressing line sizing, moisture removal, condensate removal, NCG removal, and superheat / 設計淨蒸汽配送系統：管徑、除濕、排凝結水、排不凝結氣體、過熱
  6. Apply the four piping practice examples to real-world installation scenarios / 應用四個配管實務範例於實際安裝情境

本章節內容導覽

7.7 Process & Operating Principle 7.8 Sizing the CS System 7.8.1.1 Simple CS Generator 7.8.2 Condensate Sampling 7.8.3 Materials of Construction 7.8.4 Surface Finish 7.8.5 Pretreatment 7.9 Cost Implications 7.10 Steam "Quality" 7.11 Distribution 7.11.1 Line Sizing 7.11.2 Moisture Removal 7.11.3 Condensate Removal 7.11.4 NCG Removal 7.11.5 Superheat 7.12 Four Piping Practice Examples Section Summary
7.7

Process & Operating Principle of a Sanitary CS Generator — 衛生型淨蒸汽發生器的製程與運作原理

Shell-and-tube evaporator, separator, and feedback control loop | pp. 100–101

Original Text (English) — pp. 100–101

Clean Steam is normally generated in a shell-and-tube heat exchanger evaporator. Feed water is introduced on one side of the tubes, while the heating medium is introduced on the other side. Heating of the feed water to above the boiling temperature causes the water to evaporate, producing steam. The heating medium does not come in direct contact with the feed water or with the clean steam, and is normally utility steam.

a) Operation

Clean Steam pressure is maintained by a feedback control loop, which modulates the supply steam control valve. The evaporator feed water is independently controlled using a level sensor and feed water pump.

b) Steam Supply

The utility steam supplied to the generator at typically 100–120 psig (7.0–8.5 kg/cm² gauge or 7.9–9.25 bar) must be at a higher pressure than the required clean steam pressure. The greater the differential between the utility steam pressure and the clean steam pressure, the higher the clean steam production rate. Utility steam pressure should be at least 30–40 psig higher than the clean steam pressure. Utility steam consumption will be approximately 10%–20% greater than the quantity of clean steam produced.

c) Clean Steam Pressure

Clean Steam pressure is selected by the user. Typical units are designed for pharmaceutical applications at 40–60 psig (3.75–5.1 bar).

d) Separator

Entrainment separators are normally designed to function over an optimum range of steam velocity. If the volume of steam increases substantially, carryover of endotoxins can occur. This condition can exist if the steam pressure differential significantly exceeds design conditions. The manufacturer should be consulted regarding the output of the generator at the highest possible pressure difference. An alarm and equipment shutdown is recommended.

e) Feed Water Level

The feed water level is controlled to protect against flooding of the evaporator and carryover of endotoxins by a high level alarm and subsequent shutdown. Evaporator level condition does not affect clean steam purity, but is an indication of insufficient feed water or excessive blow down.

A feed pump may be required if feed water supply pressure is inadequate. Depending on system design, a feed pressure of approximately 8–10 psig above the maximum expected Clean Steam pressure is required.

A sample cooler fitted with conductivity meter and alarms is often used to monitor Clean Steam condensate purity.

中文解說與學習重點

衛生型 CS 發生器架構

本質上是一個「淨版鍋爐」：以 utility steam 為熱源，在管殼式熱交換器中加熱純化進料水，產生淨蒸汽。關鍵設計原則：

  • 隔離原則：加熱介質與進料水/淨蒸汽「永不接觸」，避免污染交叉
  • 壓力差驅動：utility steam 須高於 CS 壓力 ≥30–40 psig，壓差越大、產量越高
  • 回饋控制：CS 壓力由控制閥自動調節 utility steam 進汽量

比喻：「雙層煮水壺」

想像一個雙層鍋：外層裝「高壓蒸汽」（utility steam），內層裝「純水」（feed water）。外層熱量透過金屬壁傳給內層，內層水沸騰產生「乾淨蒸汽」。內外層液體永遠不混合，確保淨蒸汽純度。

分離器 (Separator) 的關鍵角色

分離器不僅移除內毒素，還移除所有滴狀攜帶 (entrainment)。但它有「速度窗口」：蒸汽流速超過設計範圍時，內毒素 carryover 風險飆升。因此：

  • 必須設置高壓差報警與自動關機
  • 運轉時不得超過製造商設計壓差上限

關鍵數據整理

Utility Steam 壓力:      100–120 psig (7.9–9.25 bar)
CS 壓力 (典型):        40–60 psig (3.75–5.1 bar)
最低壓差:             ≥ 30–40 psig
Utility 消耗量:        CS 產量 × 1.10–1.20
Feed 水壓力:          CS 壓力 + 8–10 psig

思考練習

如果你的工廠 utility steam 壓力只有 80 psig，而淨蒸汽需求壓力為 50 psig，壓差僅 30 psig。這會帶來什麼問題？該如何改善？

7.8

Sizing the Clean Steam System — 淨蒸汽系統尺寸設計

Capacity calculation, diversity factor, generator selection | pp. 102–103

Original Text (English) — pp. 102–103

Figure 7-3 provides a systematic approach to sizing the Clean Steam system. The process involves identifying all use points, determining the peak demand for each, applying diversity factors, and selecting a generator with adequate capacity plus a safety margin.

7.8.1.1 CS Produced from a Simple Clean Steam Generator

There are applications where pyrogen free steam and sanitary construction features are not required, and at the same time, Utility Steam cannot be used. In such cases, it may be most economical to utilize a simple Clean Steam generator of the most economical design. Savings may be worthwhile when the elimination of the steam separator is combined with non-sanitary features such as:

  • Non-sanitary pipe and fittings
  • Non-sanitary instruments and valves
  • No polishing
  • Minimum controls

The elimination of the separator alone does not provide significant cost savings. It is important to remember that the separator’s function is more than removal of endotoxins. It removes entrainment, which includes all types of contaminants present in the feed water, except volatiles. Without an entrainment separator, impurities from the feed water may well be entrained in the steam and the moisture content can be much higher than in the standard entrained generator.

Thus the feed water purity becomes a critical factor in controlling the steam purity if entrainment is not incorporated in the design.

Independent sanitary entrainment devices are available for installation at, or close to, the point of use, and may be used with typical “Simple CS generators” as well as to control additional moisture build up due to heat losses in the distribution system of Sanitary CS Generators.

中文解說與學習重點

Sizing 基本步驟

  1. 列出所有使用點：滅菌器、凝水器、SIP 腳位等
  2. 計算尖峰需求：每個使用點的最大蒸汽消耗量
  3. 應用多樣性係數 (Diversity Factor)：不是所有使用點同時運作，典型 0.6–0.8
  4. 加安全裕量：通常 10%–20%
  5. 選型發生器：確保容量 ≥ 計算需求

Simple CS Generator 與 Sanitary CS Generator 的關鍵區別

項目Sanitary CS Gen.Simple CS Gen.
分離器有，高效移除 entrainment無，需靠 feed water 純度控制
配管/閥衛生級、316L SS非衛生級，可用碳鋼
表面處理拋光、鈍化無拋光要求
控制系統完整回饋控制最簡化控制
適用場景藥品接觸、SIP非產品接觸、但不能用 utility steam

比喻：「瀎水器」與「淨水器」

Simple CS Generator 就像一臺基本瀎水器：能燒沸水，但不能過濾雜質。如果水源本身夠乾淨，燒出來的蒸汽就沒問題。但如果水源含雜質，就需要「淨水器」（Sanitary CS Generator 的分離器）來確保蒸汽品質。

使用點獨立分離器 (Point-of-Use Separator)

配合 Simple CS Generator 的補救措施：在使用點前安裝獨立衛生級分離器。同樣可用於 Sanitary CS 系統中，補償配送管線熱損造成的額外水分。

7.8.2

Steam Condensate Sampling — 蒸汽凝結水取樣

Purity sampling (endotoxin, conductivity) and quality sampling (dryness, NCGs) | pp. 103–104

Original Text (English) — pp. 103–104

7.8.2.1 Purity Sampling

When required by the process, the steam purity shall be monitored through acceptable sampling techniques. A slipstream of the steam may be passed through a sample condenser/cooler, fitted with a sampling valve.

To ensure that the steam does not contribute to drug product contamination, sampling should be included during commissioning, as a good engineering practice, and/or prior to each time the steam is used.

If the sampling requirement is for endotoxin or pyrogen testing, the sample cooler, tubing and valve should be of sanitary construction.

Sample coolers can be fitted to the CS generator, or located in the distribution line, or at the use point (recommended location), or a combination thereof. It is common practice to fit sample coolers with conductivity monitors and alarms.

Endotoxin removal: The condensate sample from a Clean Steam generator with separator is expected to show 3–4 log10 level reduction in pyrogens compared to the level in the feed water.

7.8.2.2 Steam “Quality” Sampling

Steam “Quality” sampling may be employed to determine the level of saturation and non-condensable gases. This can be determined by applying a steam calorimeter and measuring the dryness or saturation level. A steam calorimeter measures the percentage by weight of steam in a mixture of steam and entrained water.

中文解說與學習重點

兩種取樣的目的差異

項目Purity Sampling 純度取樣Quality Sampling 品質取樣
目的確認蒸汽不污染藥品確認蒸汽是否足夠「乾」
檢測項目電導度、內毒素、TOC乾燥度、不凝結氣體 (NCG)
設備Sample cooler + 電導度計蒸汽熱量計 (calorimeter)
材質要求衛生級（測內毒素時）一般級
推薦位置使用點 (use point)系統代表性位置

3–4 log 內毒素去除：非常高的指標

3 log = 99.9% 去除；4 log = 99.99% 去除。這代表一個設計良好的帶分離器的 CS 發生器可以將進料水中的內毒素降低到非常安全的水平。

比喻：「血壓計」與「體脂機」

Purity sampling 就像量血壓：確認「成分」是否合格。Quality sampling 就像測體脂：確認「物理狀態」是否合格（蒸汽夠乾、沒有空氣）。兩者都需要，但目的不同。

實務思考

你的工廠在哪些位置安裝了 sample cooler？是在發生器出口、配送管線中、還是使用點？ISPE 推薦使用點，因為那才是蒸汽實際進入製程的狀態。

7.8.3

Materials of Construction — 材質規格

Stainless steel, titanium, and carbon steel applications | pp. 104–105

Original Text (English) — pp. 104–105

7.8.3.1 Materials for Sanitary and Simple CS Generators

Structural integrity and chemical compatibility with the contact fluid and its constituents are two of the more practical issues that drive construction material selection for CS systems.

The inherent corrosion potential forces CS manufacturers to consider relatively inert metals including stainless steel or titanium. Sanitary piping and valves, considered unnecessary for utility and simple CS generators, are often standard features for CS systems. The materials chosen should not contribute to contamination of the drug product.

Typical materials of construction:

  • Evaporator & separator — Shell, tubesheets, internals: 300 series S.S.
  • Evaporator tubes — 300 series or titanium, or other suitable alloy
  • Heat exchangers (feed heater, blow down, sample cooler) — 300 series
  • Piping — 300 series for water and CS; carbon steel for utility steam contact
  • Valves — 300 series and elastomers/diaphragms for water
  • Skid & structural — Carbon steel

7.8.3.2 Materials for Utility Steam Generation

Chemical compatibility with the utility boiler generated steam and the carried over feed water chemicals are required. Based on particulate levels and required steam purity, more than one filtration stage may be utilized. Acceptable materials must be relatively inert and may include SS or tin-coated copper.

中文解說與學習重點

材質選擇兩大原則

  • 結構強度：耐受蒸汽壓力與溫度
  • 化學相容性：不與淨蒸汽/凝結水反應、不污染藥品

淨蒸汽本身極具腐蝕性（高純水+高溫），因此必須使用耐腐蝕材料。

材質整理表

部件衛生型 CSUtility Steam 應用
蒸發器/分離器300 系 SSN/A
蒸發器管300 系 SS 或鈰合金N/A
配管300 系 SSSS 或錫鍍銅
Utility steam 側碳鋼碳鋼
機架/結構碳鋼碳鋼

為什麼用鈰？

鈰合金在蒸發器管子中的優勢：耐腐蝕性比不鏽鋼更佳，尤其對氯化物的抗性。但成本較高，通常在進料水中可能含有微量氯化物的應用中才會選用。

7.8.4

Surface Finish — 表面處理

Mechanical polishing, electropolishing, passivation | p. 104

Original Text (English) — p. 104

Mechanical polishing (MP), electropolishing (EP), and passivation processes are implemented in some stainless CS systems. Chlorine and/or chlorides will damage the generator regardless of the finish.

The operating temperatures of these systems are more than sufficient for inhibiting microbiological growth. Therefore, MP is advocated for final finishing of mechanical welds, with mill finishes and final passivation to optimize the formation of the corrosion resistant chromium oxide barrier. Electropolishing will also optimize the chromium oxide barrier, and should be considered if passivation is not an option.

中文解說與學習重點

表面處理的目的

淨蒸汽系統的表面處理主要目的不是抑菌（因為運作溫度已足夠殺菌），而是：

  • 防腐蝕：通過形成 Cr₂O₃ 氧化鉻保護層
  • 處理焊接痕跡：MP 應用於機械焊接的最終處理

處理方式選擇

處理方式作用適用時機
Mill Finish + Passivation工廠原始表面 + 鈍化增強 Cr₂O₃首選方案
Mechanical Polish (MP)研磨平整焊接處焊接後處理
Electropolish (EP)電化學拋光，最佳化 Cr₂O₃當鈍化無法執行時

氯的致命威脅

無論表面處理多好，氯和氯化物都會摧毀發生器。這是整個 CS 系統最大的化學威脅，必須在進料水前處理階段徹底移除。

7.8.5

Pretreatment for CS Generators — 淨蒸汽發生器前處理

Scale, corrosion, and volatile carryover considerations | pp. 105–106

Original Text (English) — pp. 105–106

The feed water pretreatment for a CS generator is born from three separate and distinct considerations:

  1. Scale formation
  2. Corrosion
  3. Volatiles which carryover with the steam and may affect steam purity

7.8.5.1 Scale

Scale formation is a function of generator feed water chemistry, concentration (depends on blow down rate), and temperature. It is independent of design and make. Because scale inhibitors are not used, and because of the relatively high operating temperatures, the TDS of the feed water should be very low. Silica is of particular concern — most manufacturers stipulate less than 1 ppm; some go as high as 5 ppm. The feed water should have no measurable hardness. It is therefore common to use DI or reverse osmosis as pretreatment.

Using compendial water as feed is wasteful, unless the steam quantity is small and the cost of a dedicated feed water pretreatment system exceeds the cost of using compendial water.

Some manufacturers offer generators to operate on softened water. Usually, the rate of blow down is increased to maintain low concentration.

Note: If the TDS of the soft water is relatively high, soft scale (such as sodium scale and sludge) can form.

7.8.5.2 Corrosion

The most common cause of corrosion is free chlorine, not chlorides. Chlorine and chlorides, at any detectable level, are very detrimental to stainless steel. The higher the temperature and chlorine level, the more severe is the attack. Chlorine is known to migrate and concentrate in localized cells where the level can reach tens or hundreds of ppm.

Chlorine can be removed from the feed water by chemical injection of a reducing agent such as sodium bisulfate, or by passing the chlorinated water through carbon filters.

7.8.5.3 Volatiles

Dissolved gases and substances that are volatile at the operating temperature of the CS generator will carryover with the steam. If such substances are objectionable, they must be removed at the pretreatment stage. Ammonia and CO₂ are examples of volatile gases that will have an effect on the conductivity, such that a condensate sample may not meet USP requirements for Purified or WFI Water.

中文解說與學習重點

前處理三大課題

CS 發生器的進料水前處理必須同時解決三個問題，缺一不可：

  • 水垢 (Scale)：降低 TDS & 矽，零硬度
  • 腐蝕 (Corrosion)：移除游離氯
  • 揮發性物質 (Volatiles)：移除 NH₃、CO₂

水垢問題詳解

淨蒸汽發生器不能使用水垢抑制劑（因為會污染蒸汽），因此進料水本身必須非常乾淨：

  • TDS 要非常低（通常使用 DI 或 RO 水）
  • 矽含量 < 1 ppm（嚴格）至 < 5 ppm（寬鬆）
  • 硬度 = 0（無可測量硬度）

即使用軟化水，高 TDS 仍可能形成鈉垢和污泥。

氯的腐蝕機制 — 深層解說

氯的危害最大難點在於「局部濃縮」：即使進料水中氯含量很低（<1 ppm），氯會遷移到縫隙、死角處濃縮到數十至數百 ppm，造成劇烈局部腐蝕。解決方法：

  • 化學注入還原劑（如亞硫酸氫鈉）
  • 活性碳過濾

比喻：「食物過敏原」

對 CS 發生器而言，氯就像人體的過敏原 — 即使微量也能觸發嚴重反應。而揮發性物質（NH₃、CO₂）就像「食物氣味」，會跟著蒸汽一起走，影響最終產品的「味道」（電導度）。

實務檢查清單

  • 你的工廠 CS 發生器進料水是用什麼？DI？RO？還是軟化水？
  • 是否定期監測進料水的矽含量和游離氯？
  • 發生器是否有定期除垢與目視檢查的 SOP？
  • Blow down rate 是多少？是否足以控制濃縮倍數？
7.9

Cost Implications — 成本考量

Economics of pharmaceutical steam production | p. 106

Original Text (English) — p. 106

Determining the economics of pharmaceutical steam production is complex. Costs are quite predictable, but vary greatly depending on scale of operation, system design, actual usage, etc.

The total operating cost to produce pharmaceutical steams is obtained by adding the cost of feed water to the costs of pretreatment and final treatment (primary ion removal and polishing).

The type of pharmaceutical steam system design option selected is typically based on feed water TDS, silica and hardness levels, organic and colloidal content, as well as anticipated steam system utility costs (acid, caustic, salt, power, and source water). Consideration should also be given to maintenance requirements and available resources.

中文解說與學習重點

成本組成

藥用蒸汽的總成本 = 進料水成本 + 前處理成本 + 最終處理成本

  • 進料水：市政水/井水成本
  • 前處理：軟化、RO、DI 等設備與耗材
  • 最終處理：離子交換、拋光等
  • 運行成本：酸、鹼、鹽、電力、源水
  • 維護成本：定期除垢、鈍化、改管等

成本決策的關鍵

進料水水質越差，前處理成本越高。但用藥典級水（WFI）作為進料水通常浪費，除非蒸汽用量很小。最佳實務是：專用 RO/DI 系統供水給 CS 發生器，與 WFI 系統分開。

7.10

Steam “Quality” — 蒸汽「品質」（乾燥度）

Dryness fraction, saturation, and moisture sources | p. 106

Original Text (English) — p. 106

Steam quality is defined as the saturation percentage of steam to water or more explicitly, the ratio of the vapor mass to the mass of the steam mixture.

Dry Saturated Steam with minimum superheat is necessary for efficient steam sterilization.

Water can be generated and carried by steam within distribution systems in two ways:

  1. In suspension as moisture when the steam is not 100% saturated
  2. As condensate separated from the steam

Water vapor carried in suspension may be reduced by: adding more heat or raising the temperature, reducing the pressure, or adding a steam entrainment separator. Water moisture and condensate may be reduced by steam traps.

中文解說與學習重點

Steam Quality 的定義

蒸汽品質（乾燥度）= 蒸汽質量 ÷ （蒸汽質量 + 水質量）

  • 100% = 乾飽和蒸汽：理想狀態，滅菌效率最高
  • <100% = 濕蒸汽：攜帶水分，滅菌效率降低
  • >100% = 過熱蒸汽：熱交換效率差，也不利滅菌

公式：蒸汽乾燥度

Steam Quality (x) = m_steam / (m_steam + m_water)

其中：
  m_steam = 蒸汽質量
  m_water = 攜帶的水分質量

理想值：x ≥ 0.95 (即 ≥ 95% 乾燥度)

比喻：「濕毛巾烘衣服」

想像用毛巾擦乾衣服：如果毛巾本身是濕的（濕蒸汽），擦來擦去衣服也乾不了。只有乾毛巾（乾飽和蒸汽）才能有效地「擦乾」（滅菌）。但如果用烘乾機（過熱蒸汽），溫度太高反而可能傷衣料。

減少蒸汽中水分的方法

  • 提高溫度/熱量：讓攜帶水分蒸發
  • 降壓：蒸汽膨脹，相對飽和度上升
  • 安裝分離器：機械方式移除液滴
  • 安裝蒸汽陀 (Steam Trap)：排出已分離的凝結水
7.11

Distribution — 淨蒸汽配送系統

Materials, connections, traps, and general design practices | pp. 106–107

Original Text (English) — pp. 106–107

Distribution systems for clean steam follow the same good engineering practices commonly used for utility steam, with the exception that contact materials must be inert to the aggressive nature of clean steam.

Corrosion-resistant 304, 316, or 316L grade stainless steel “tubing” or solid-drawn “pipe” are commonly used. Surface finish is not critical due to the self-sanitizing nature of the clean steam. Mill finish or 180-grit mechanically polished pipe or tubing is sufficient. TIG Orbital welding and post-installation passivation is considered appropriate.

Piping must be designed to allow for thermal expansion and to drain condensate.

Note: Drains should have air breaks.

Sanitary clamps or pipe flanges are most commonly used where the piping must be broken, but welded connections are used as much as possible to eliminate maintenance costs and potential for leaks. Threaded connections may be suitable for instrumentation if positioned to drain condensate and remain hot.

Ball valves are commonly used for isolation because elastomeric diaphragms do not hold up well in this service. Where diaphragm valves are used, Teflon-encapsulated EPDM diaphragms give the best long-term performance.

Steam quality sampling may be determined during “commissioning” and consistency ensured based on the proper location and subsequent maintenance of traps, entrainment separators, and vents.

中文解說與學習重點

CS 配送與 Utility Steam 配送的異同

相同點：都遵循良好工程實務 — 排凝結水、排空氣、支撐管線、熱膨脹補償。

不同點：CS 配送必須全程使用耐腐蝕材料（304/316/316L SS），因為淨蒸汽極具腐蝕性。

CS 配送設計重點

項目規格
管材304/316/316L SS tubing 或 solid-drawn pipe
表面處理Mill finish 或 180-grit MP 即可
焊接TIG Orbital welding + 安裝後鈍化
連接優先焊接；需拆卸處用 Sanitary clamp
閥類Ball valve（首選）；Diaphragm 用 Teflon-EPDM
注意排水口需設空氣間隔 (air break)

為什麼用 Ball Valve 而不是 Diaphragm Valve？

想像在炎熱環境中使用橡膠手套與金屬工具：橡膠手套（橡膠隔膜）很快就會老化變脫，而金屬工具（金屬球閥）則能長期耐受高溫。CS 配送溫度高、腐蝕性強，橡膠隔膜壽命明顯縮短。

7.11.1

Line Sizing — 管徑選擇

Maximum velocity and erosion control | p. 107

Original Text (English) — p. 107

The steam distribution header should be sized for a maximum velocity of 7,200 feet per minute (120 ft/sec or 37 m/sec) to limit erosion and extend the life expectancy of the piping. Condensate line sizing should follow good engineering practices for utility condensate.

中文解說與學習重點

關鍵設計數據

蒸汽幹管最大流速:
  7,200 ft/min = 120 ft/sec = 37 m/sec

超過此速度 → 管壁侵蝕加速 → 管線壽命縮短

實務意義

這個速度限制是避免「高速侵蝕」的設計上限。在實際設計中：

  • 根據尖峰流量計算所需管徑
  • 確保在最大負荷時流速 ≤ 37 m/s
  • 凝結水管按一般 utility condensate 標準設計
7.11.2

Water Moisture Removal — 除濕/去水分

Pressure reduction, in-line separators | p. 107

Original Text (English) — p. 107

Water vapor forms in steam systems due to heat loss, causing a change in the liquid/vapor ratio or steam “quality”.

Steam may be dried of moisture by reducing the generated pressure just prior to the point of use to coincide with the steam temperature of saturation at the reduced pressure.

Moisture entrained in the steam can also be removed by installing an In-Line separator at the point of use, just prior to, or just after, the regulator. If the separator is located upstream of the regulator, the regulator should be protected from water damage (wire drawing) and impingement damage on the regulator diaphragms.

In-line separators are available in sizes from 1/2" to 6" (approx. 1 cm to 15 cm) and remove moisture with a series of baffles on which the suspended water droplets impinge and fall out by gravity to the drain, which must be piped to a trap. Separators have a separation efficiency of better than 99% in the removal of all liquid and solid entrainment exceeding 10 microns.

中文解說與學習重點

兩種除濕方法

  • 方法 1：降壓 — 在使用點前降低蒸汽壓力，讓蒸汽在新的壓力下達到飽和狀態，多餘的熱量讓攜帶水分蒸發
  • 方法 2：線內分離器 — 利用擋板 (baffles) 使水滴撞擊並靠重力落下，效率 >99%（>10 μm）

分離器安裝位置的註意事項

如果分離器安裝在調壓閥 (regulator) 上游，必須保護調壓閥免受：

  • Wire drawing：水流沖刷閥座造成的損傷
  • Impingement damage：水滴撞擊隔膜造成的損壞

因此許多設計將分離器放在調壓閥下游。

7.11.3

Condensate Removal — 凝結水排除

Steam traps, water hammer prevention, bacterial contamination | pp. 107–108

Original Text (English) — pp. 107–108

Condensate is the water that has separated from the liquid vapor mixture and forms in steam systems due to heat losses and natural separation effects. Lines should be designed to prevent the buildup of condensate to avoid dangerous water hammer and to eliminate potential cold spots where bacteria can grow.

Any untrapped vertical length of pipe will quickly fill with condensate. If this condensate is allowed to stand for sufficient time, it can cool and become a breeding ground for bacteria. This bacteria could possibly be entrained back into the main distribution header and contaminate use points downstream.

Worst case condensate removal locations should be sampled monthly for presence of bacteria.

The following practices are commonly employed:

  • Each line is adequately supported to avoid sagging and subsequent condensate accumulation.
  • Steam traps are installed at all points where condensate can collect (e.g., at least every 100 feet (30 meters) of line, upstream of control valves, at the bottom of vertical risers, etc.). Steam traps for clean steam service should be sanitary design and self-draining.
  • If the main distribution header is above the use points, the branches should be routed from the top of the header to avoid excessive condensate loads at the branch.
  • An alternative is to run the main distribution header below the use points. Then the branches can drain back to the main header, avoiding the need for additional traps.
  • The requirement to trap each branch can be waived for short drops from main headers to vessels that are in frequent use where sterilization and water hammer are not impacted.
中文解說與學習重點

凝結水的三大風險

  • 水錘 (Water Hammer)：凝結水被蒸汽推動撞擊管壁，可能造成管線破裂
  • 細菌滋生：冷卻的凝結水是微生物溫床，細菌可能被帶回主管
  • 滅菌失敗：冷點無法達到滅菌溫度

凝結水管理實務規則

規則內容
管線支撐避免垮陷，防止凝結水積累
Steam Trap 位置每30m、控制閥前、垂直升管底部
Trap 規格衛生級、自排式設計
支管走向幹管在上方 → 支管從頂部接出
備選方案幹管在下方 → 支管自然回流，減少 trap
豁免條件短落管 + 頻繁使用的設備
取樣最差位置每月採樣檢測細菌

比喻：「屋頂排水溝」

蒸汽管線的凝結水管理就像屋頂排水：如果排水溝垮陷或堵塞，水就會積在屋頂上，最終造成漏水（水錘）或發霉（細菌）。良好的斜度設計和定期清理（steam trap）是關鍵。

實務檢查

你的工廠的 CS 管線是幹管在上還是在下？支管是從幹管頂部還是側面接出？每個 steam trap 是否定期檢查（卡死的 trap 會排不出凝結水，洩漏的 trap 會排掉蒸汽）？

7.11.4

Non-Condensable Gas Removal — 不凝結氣體排除

Air as insulator, thermostatic traps, correct piping | p. 108

Original Text (English) — p. 108

Air and other non-condensable gases should be minimized from pharmaceutical steam systems. Since air acts as an insulator, incomplete sterilization can occur in the process. Air in a system offers a very effective barrier to the heat transfer which will lead to a reduced temperature at the surface of a tube, system component or process equipment.

Air can be discharged using steam traps, however excessive levels may slow down the discharge of condensate. The subcooled condensate can then lead to insufficient sterilization temperatures due to the excess water.

The removal of air can be achieved by placing thermostatic pharmaceutical steam traps with the inlet in the upward position. These should be placed in positions where air is prone to collect such as:

  • The terminal points of the main and large branches of the steam header
  • High points in tanks, reactors and sterilizers

In the case of air and condensate discharge at the bottom of large vessels, the air and condensate should be separated by correct piping practices.

中文解說與學習重點

空氣為什麼是滅菌的敵人？

空氣是優良的「隔熱層」，會在被滅菌表面形成氣袋，阻擋蒸汽接觸該表面。結果：

  • 該區域溫度達不到滅菌要求
  • 造成「冷點」，滅菌不完全
  • 嚴重時導致整個 SIP 失敗

NCG 排除策略

  • 設備：熱靜力式 (thermostatic) 藥用蒸汽陀，進口朝上安裝
  • 位置：幹管與大支管的末端、槽體/反應器/滅菌器的高點
  • 大容器底部：需將空氣與凝結水通過正確配管分開

比喻：「池塘的水面膜」

空氣在蒸汽系統中就像池塘表面的一層膜 — 看不見但會阻止熱量穿透。就像在澡缸裡放了一層保鲜膜，炎水的熱量就無法有效傳遞到被滅菌的物品。

7.11.5

Superheat — 過熱

Impact on sterilization efficiency | p. 108

Original Text (English) — p. 108

While higher-pressure steam can be used to compensate for superheat, the latent heat, or killing power of the potentially superheated steam is reduced at higher pressures; leading to increased sterilization cycles.

中文解說與學習重點

過熱蒸汽的問題

過熱蒸汽溫度高於該壓力下的飽和溫度，看似「更熱」，但實際上：

  • 潛熱 (Latent Heat) 減少：過熱蒸汽的熱交換主要靠顯熱，而非相變潛熱，效率較低
  • 滅菌時間增加：需要更長的滅菌週期
  • 為什麼高壓更糟：壓力越高，飽和蒸汽的潛熱越低，滅菌效率越差

比喻：「燒紅的鐵塊」vs「蒸籠」

過熱蒸汽就像用燒紅的鐵塊去蒸食物 — 雖然溫度很高，但熱量傳遞效率遠不如蒸籠（飽和蒸汽）。蒸籠靠的是蒸汽凝結釋放潛熱，這才是最高效的熱傳遞方式。

7.12

Four Examples of Correct Piping Practice — 四個正確配管實務範例

Practical installation guidance from Figure 7-4 | pp. 109–110

Original Text (English) — pp. 109–110

Figure 7-4 (Vessel Sterilization) and the four piping practice examples illustrate the correct configuration for clean steam distribution. These examples integrate the principles discussed in Sections 7.11.1 through 7.11.5:

Example 1: Branch Take-Off from Header

When the main distribution header is above the use points, branches should be taken from the top of the header. This prevents condensate in the header from flowing directly into the branch line. Each branch must be trapped at its lowest point.

Example 2: Header Below Use Points

An alternative design runs the main distribution header below the use points. Branches rise upward to the equipment, and condensate naturally drains back to the main header by gravity, reducing the need for individual branch traps.

Example 3: Vessel Bottom Drain

For large vessels, air and condensate must be discharged at the bottom. A properly designed piping arrangement separates the air discharge (via thermostatic trap with inlet up) from the condensate discharge (via standard steam trap). This prevents air pockets from impeding condensate drainage.

Example 4: Short Drop to Frequently Used Equipment

For short vertical drops from a main header to equipment that is used daily (e.g., a media storage tank sterilized daily), individual traps on the branch can be waived. The condensate built up in the vertical drop has limited time to cool, and is quickly eliminated by the trap at the bottom of the vessel when the block valve is opened. The vertical drop is sterilized daily with the vessel.

中文解說與學習重點

四個範例總覽

#情境核心原則
1幹管在上、使用點在下支管從頂部接出 + 底部裝 trap
2幹管在下、使用點在上凝結水自然回流，減少 trap 數量
3大容器底部排放分開排空氣與排凝結水的管路
4短落管至頻繁使用的設備可豁免支管 trap，每日滅菌即可

範例 1 詳解：支管從頂部接出

這是最常見的配置。如果支管從幹管側面或底部接出，幹管中的凝結水會直接流入支管，增加水錘與污染風險。從頂部接出，只有乾蒸汽進入支管。

範例 3 詳解：空氣與凝結水分離

大容器底部同時有空氣和凝結水需要排出。如果共用同一管路，空氣會堵塞 trap，導致凝結水排不出去。因此必須：

  • 熱靜力式 trap（進口朝上）負責排空氣
  • 標準 steam trap 負責排凝結水

範例 4 詳解：短落管豁免條件

只有在以下條件都滿足時才能豁免：

  • 落管距離短
  • 設備每天都在使用（如每日滅菌）
  • 凝結水量不足以影響水錘和滅菌

綜合思考練習

在你的工廠中，CS 配送管線是哪種配置？請逐一檢查：

  • 支管是否從幹管頂部接出？
  • 每個 steam trap 是否正常運作？
  • 大容器底部是否有獨立的排氣與排水管路？
  • 有哪些「短落管」被豁免了 trap？豁免條件是否真的滿足？

Section Summary 本節摘要

本節涵蓋了淨蒸汽系統從生成到配送的完整知識體系：

  • 發生器運作：Sanitary CS Generator 以管殼式熱交換器+分離器+回饋控制產生高純度淨蒸汽；Simple CS Generator 省略分離器和衛生級特徵，適用於非產品接觸場景
  • Sizing：識別使用點→尖峰需求→多樣性係數→安全裕量→選型
  • 取樣：Purity sampling（電導度/內毒素）與 Quality sampling（乾燥度/NCG）必須分開考慮
  • 材質與表面：300 系 SS/鈰為主，鈍化或 EP 優化 Cr₂O₃ 保護層，氯是最大威脅
  • 前處理：三大課題：水垢（低TDS/矽/零硬度）、腐蝕（除氯）、揮發性物質（除NH₃/CO₂）
  • 蒸汽品質：乾飽和蒸汽最佳，過熱蒸汽反而降低滅菌效率
  • 配送：304/316L SS、≤37 m/s 流速、Ball valve 首選、分離器+Steam trap 組合除濕排凝
  • NCG 排除：空氣是隔熱層，必須用熱靜力式 trap 在高點和末端排除
  • 四個配管範例：支管頂部接出、幹管在下自然回流、大容器排氣排水分離、短落管豁免
8

Chapter 8: Storage & Distribution

儲存與分配系統 | p111-p145

ISPE Baseline® Guide Volume 4: Water and Steam Systems | p111-p135

Learning Objectives | 學習目標

  • Understand the role of storage tanks and key design considerations (capacity, location, type) | 瞭解儲水槽的角色及關鍵設計考量
  • Compare eight common distribution configurations using the Decision Tree | 透過決策樹比較八種常見的配水系統架構
  • Evaluate trade-offs of each configuration (microbial control, energy, cost, complexity) | 評估各架構在微生物控制、能源、成本與複雜度之間的取捨
  • Compare stainless steel vs. PVDF as materials of construction | 比較不銹鋼與 PVDF 作為管材的優缺點
  • Identify key system components: heat exchangers, vent filters, pumps, valves | 辨識主要系統元件及其設計要求
  • Compare storage tank vs. tankless WFI systems | 比較有儲水槽與無儲水槽 WFI 系統的優劣
Original Text

8.1 Introduction

This chapter provides an overview of eight common distribution configurations and a decision tree to help decide which system best suits the operating requirements. A comparison of tank versus tankless systems is addressed, as well as alternative materials of construction available, and ancillary equipment related to overall distribution systems. Common industry practices are listed as examples, to help clarify regulatory requirements.

Commentary | 解說

8.1 導論

章節架構總覽

本章是 ISPE Volume 4 中最具實務價值的章節之一，涵蓋了從「水怎麼存」到「水怎麼送」的完整設計思維。核心內容包括：

  • 8 種配水系統架構 — 從批次式到連續式，從熱儲存到常溫儲存
  • 決策樹 — 幫助工程師根據使用需求選擇最佳架構
  • 材料比較 — 不銹鋼 vs. PVDF 的全面對比
  • 系統元件 — 熱交換器、呼吸過濾器、泵浦、閥門的設計要點

比喻：城市供水系統

想像一座製藥廠就像一座小型城市 — 儲水槽是水塔，配水迴路是自來水管線，使用點是各家各戶的水龍頭。設計的核心挑戰就是：如何確保每個水龍頭打開時，都能得到符合品質要求的水，同時不浪費太多能源和金錢。

Original Text

8.2 System Design

8.2.1 General Considerations

A storage system is used to accommodate peak flow requirements against usage rates. The storage system must maintain the feed water quality to ensure the appropriate quality of the end use of product. Storage allows a smaller, less costly pretreatment system to meet peak demand. A smaller treatment system operates closer to the ideal of continuous, dynamic flow. Large manufacturing sites, or systems serving different buildings, may use storage tanks to separate one section of the loop, and others to minimize cross contamination.

The main disadvantage of a storage tank is its capital cost, and the cost of associated pumps, vent filters, and instrumentation. However, this is usually less than the increased cost of pretreatment equipment sized to handle the peak use rate in the facility.

Another disadvantage of storage is that it introduces a region of slow moving water, which can promote bacterial growth.

8.2.2 Capacity

Criteria affecting storage capacity include the user's demand profile or the amount of use, duration, timing, and diversity, (in the case of more than one user), balance between the supply of pre- and final-treated waters, and whether the system is recirculating or non-recirculating. Careful consideration of these criteria will affect cost and water quality.

The storage tank must provide reserve to minimize cycling of the treatment equipment and to reduce pump cavitation. It should provide sufficient reserve to enable routine maintenance and orderly system shutdown in the event of an emergency, which can vary from few to many hours, depending on the size and configuration of the system and maintenance procedures.

8.2.3 Storage Tank Location

It may not be cost-effective to locate storage tanks as close as possible to the point of use, within high-cost, GMP-finished areas. It may be more advantageous to locate them close to the generation equipment, for ease of maintenance. Utility areas are acceptable for this purpose, if access is provided (and the area is kept clean).

8.2.4 Types of Storage Tanks

Vertical storage tanks are common but horizontal tanks may be necessary if overhead space is limited. For recirculating systems, tank design should include an internal spray ball to ensure that all interior surfaces are wetted for microbial control. Jacketing is usually provided in hot systems, to maintain water temperature over long periods without makeup, or to temper high influent temperatures, to preclude excessive rouging and pump cavitation.

To avoid the absorption of carbon dioxide and its effect on conductivity, inert blanketing of the tank headspace should be considered. Storage tanks must be fitted with a sub-micron hydrophobic vent filter to reduce bio-burden and particles.

The maximum size of a single storage vessel is often limited by the space available in the facility. It may be necessary to resort to multiple tanks to obtain the desired capacity. In this case, interconnecting piping must be carefully designed to assure adequate flow through all supply and return branches.

Commentary | 解說

8.2 系統設計

儲水槽的核心價值

儲水槽的主要功能是緩衝（buffering）— 將前處理系統的穩定產水與生產端的瞬間大量用水需求解耦。沒有儲水槽，前處理系統必須能應對最大瞬時需求，設備會非常昂貴。

  • 優點：降低前處理設備規模、提供應急儲備、分區隔離
  • 缺點：資本成本高、引入低流速死水區域、增加微生物風險

比喻：水庫 vs. 即時供水

儲水槽就像是一個小型水庫 — 雖然能穩定供水，但靜止的水更容易滋生微生物。就像家裡的熱水器，如果長時間不用，裡面的水質會變差。所以設計重點是：如何讓「水庫」裡的水保持流動和乾淨。

容量設計考量要點

  • 用水需求分析：用量、時間、頻率、同時使用的多寡
  • 供需平衡：前處理產水速率 vs. 使用點消耗速率
  • 應急儲備：維護停機和緊急情況所需的最低水量
  • 泵浦保護：足夠水位避免泵浦氣蝕（cavitation）

儲水槽設計要點

  • Spray ball：噴淋球確保內表面全部潤濕，防止乾燥區域滋生微生物
  • 夾套加熱：熱水系統必備，維持溫度防止 rouge 生成
  • 氮氣覆蓋：防止 CO₂ 吸收導致電導率上升
  • 呼吸過濾器：疏水性、0.1-0.2 微米，必備！
  • 位置：不需要放在 GMP 潔淨區，公用設施區即可
Original Text

8.3 System Distribution Design

8.3.1 General Considerations

Proper design of both the water storage and distribution systems is critical to the success of a pharmaceutical water system.

The optimal design of any water storage and distribution system must accomplish three things:

  1. Maintain the quality of the water within acceptable limits.
  2. Deliver the water to the use points at the required flow rate and temperature.
  3. Minimize capital and operating expenses.

Although items 2 and 3 are well understood, item 1 is often misinterpreted. It is not necessary to protect the water from every form of degradation, only to maintain the quality within acceptable limits. For instance, water stored in the presence of air absorbs CO2, increasing the conductivity. This degradation can be avoided by blanketing the storage vessel with nitrogen. However, for many systems this would be a wasteful expenditure if the increased conductivity were still within the required specification.

As technology has improved over the years, many design features such as storage at elevated temperature, constant circulation, use of sanitary connections, polished tubing, orbital welding, frequent sanitization, and the use of diaphragm valves have become common place. To incorporate all of these features into each new design typically leads to ever escalating costs with little if any reduction in risk of contamination.

A more reasonable approach is to utilize design features that provide the greatest reduction in contamination risk at the most reasonable cost. The idea of selecting design features based on "return" on investment where "return" is defined as reduction in contamination risk, can be very helpful in controlling system cost and in evaluating different alternatives.

EXAMPLE: A USP compendial water system is designed with a 316L SS storage and distribution system and operates normally at 80°C. The tubing is all sanitary, orbital welded, with minimal clamps and zero dead leg diaphragm valves. Water is kept circulating at a minimum return velocity of 3ft/sec. In this case, use of high mechanical polish tubing (<20 Ra) with electropolishing may not be required. The risk of contamination is already low, and the impact of this upgraded surface finish is questionable.

However, if the same system were open to the atmosphere, consideration would be given to installing a 0.2 micron vent filter on the storage vessel, as the reduction in contamination risk is quite large for a relatively small investment.

As a general rule, a water system is optimized as a result of:

  1. Minimizing the time the water is held at conditions which favor growth
  2. Minimizing changes to water temperature
  3. Contacting all areas during sanitization
Commentary | 解說

8.3 配水系統設計

配水設計三大目標

  1. 維持水質在規格範圍內 — 注意：不是追求「零污染」，而是「在可接受範圍內」
  2. 在正確的流量和溫度下供水 — 使用點的需求規格
  3. 最小化資本和運營成本 — 全生命週期成本

重要觀念：風險導向設計（Risk-based Design）

這段話是 ISPE 非常重要的設計哲學 — 不要盲目堆疊所有最先進的設計功能。每個設計功能都有成本，應該評估其「投資報酬率」（ROI），其中「報酬」是指「降低污染風險的程度」。

範例中的 80°C 迴路系統已經有足夠的微生物控制，再加上電拋光的邊際效益很小。但如果系統開放於大氣，加裝 0.2μm 呼吸過濾器的 ROI 就非常高。

比喻：保險的概念

這就像買保險 — 你不需要把所有險種都買齊。如果你住在沒有地震的地方，買地震險就是浪費。但如果你住在海邊，洪水險的 ROI 就非常高。設計水系統也是一樣，要根據實際風險來選擇設計功能。

系統最佳化三大原則

  • 最小化水停留在利於微生物生長條件的時間 — 溫度 15-55°C 是危險區
  • 最小化水溫變化 — 每次變溫都消耗能源且可能產生溫度死角
  • 消毒時接觸所有區域 — 沒有死角，每個表面都要被消毒到
Original Text

8.3.2 Distribution Design Concepts

The two basic concepts developed for distribution of pharmaceutical waters are referred to as the "batch" and "dynamic/continuous" distribution concepts.

The batch concept utilizes at least two storage tanks. While one is being filled, the other is in service providing pharmaceutical waters to the various process users. After one tank has been filled from the water final treatment system, it is isolated and the water inside is tested. Only after testing is that tank put into service. The water is often drained after 24 hours, but can be validated for longer periods of time.

The dynamic/continuous concept off-sets the peak instantaneous water demand through utilization of a single water storage vessel which simultaneously receives final pretreatment system make-up, stores the water in the vessel, and ultimately supplies it to the various process users while maintaining water quality.

The advantage of the "batch" distribution concept is that the water is tested before use with tank QA/QC lot release. The advantages of the "dynamic/continuous" distribution concept include lower lifecycle costs, less complex piping around the storage vessel, and a much more efficient operation.

Once a system distribution concept has been selected, the following additional considerations should be evaluated:

  • System configuration including whether series or parallel loops are required, distribution loop points of use, cooling requirements, reheat requirements, secondary loop tanks versus tankless considerations
  • Hot (65-80°C), cold (4-10°C), or ambient temperature process use point requirements
  • Sanitization method (steam, hot water, ozone, or chemical)
Commentary | 解說

8.3.2 配水設計概念

兩大配水概念比較

特性 批次式（Batch） 動態/連續式（Dynamic）
儲水槽數量 至少 2 個 1 個
品質確認 先測試再使用（QA 放行） 線上即時監控
管路複雜度 較高 較低
生命週期成本 較高 較低
適用場景 產水設備不穩定、需 QA 放行 現代標準設計

實務觀察

現代製藥水系統幾乎都採用動態/連續式設計。批次式是早期產水設備不可靠時的做法，現在有了線上 conductivity 和 TOC 監測，即時品質確認已經可以取代離線 QA 測試。批次式主要只在特殊合規要求下才會見到。

配水系統選型三大考量

  • 溫度需求：使用點需要幾度的水？Hot (65-80°C)、Cold (4-10°C)、Ambient？
  • 迴路架構：單迴路？平行迴路？串聯子迴路？
  • 消毒方法：蒸汽、熱水、臭氧、化學消毒 — 這會反向影響材料選擇
Original Text

8.3.3 Distribution Decision Tree

The decision tree in Figure 8-1 is presented to aid in the analysis of distribution design. Most of the systems in use today are represented by one of these eight configurations, but other designs may also be acceptable.

In evaluating which configuration is optimal for a given situation, the designer needs to consider many factors, including:

  • Requirements for Quality Assurance release
  • Desired specification of water (DI, USP WFI, etc.)
  • Hydraulic limitations
  • Required temperature at each drop
  • Number of use points
  • Cost of energy

Decision tree guide (8 configurations):

  1. Batched System
  2. Branched / One Way
  3. Parallel Loops, Single Tank
  4. Hot Storage, Hot Distribution
  5. Ambient Storage, Ambient Distribution
  6. Hot Storage, Cool and Reheat
  7. Hot Tank, Self-contained Distribution
  8. Point of Use Heat Exchanger

Each configuration varies in the degree of microbial control provided and in the amount of energy required. Better microbial control is usually achieved by minimizing the amount of time water is exposed to conditions favoring microbial growth.

Configurations that store water at sanitizing conditions such as hot, under ozone, or circulation at turbulent velocities, are expected to provide better microbial control than those that do not. Naturally, hot circulating systems are more forgiving than cold systems from a microbiological perspective.

Energy usage is minimized by limiting the amount of water changing temperature. Configurations storing water hot but supplying it at lower temperature must cool the water before use. Configurations that constantly cool and reheat water utilize more energy than systems that do not.

Usually the cooling medium is tower water since this is the least expensive to generate. In most parts of the world, tower water is not cold enough to allow use temperatures much below 25°C. A second cooling exchanger using chilled water or glycol must be added if the required use temperature is below 25°C.

Commentary | 解說

8.3.3 配水決策樹

決策樹邏輯流程（Figure 8-1 描述）

ISPE 的決策樹幫助工程師透過回答一系列問題來選擇最佳架構：

  • 是否需要 QA 放行？ → Yes = 批次式系統
  • 使用點數量少且可頻繁沖洗？ → Yes = 分支/單向式
  • 需要多種溫度？ → Yes = 平行迴路
  • 所有使用點都需要熱水？ → Yes = 熱儲存/熱配送
  • 水在常溫產出且使用？ → Yes = 常溫儲存/常溫配送
  • 需要嚴格微生物控制且無暇消毒？ → Yes = 熱儲存/冷卻再加熱
  • 能源成本是關鍵？ → Yes = 熱槽/自含式配送
  • 少量低溫使用點？ → Yes = 使用點熱交換器

微生物控制 vs. 能源消耗的取捨

這是配水設計的核心矛盾：

  • 最佳微生物控制 = 持續高溫 (80°C)，但能源成本最高
  • 最低能源成本 = 常溫，但微生物控制最困難
  • 折衷方案 = 熱儲存 + 使用點冷卻，或常溫 + 定期消毒

冷卻設計實務提醒

從 80°C 冷卻到使用溫度：

  • >25°C：冷卻水塔水即可（最經濟）
  • <25°C：需要第二段冷凍水/glycol 冷卻
  • 全程用冷凍水從 80°C 降到 <25°C 不划算 — 冰機規模太大
Original Text

8.3.4 Example System Descriptions

The following describes the systems, contained in the accompanying decision tree, that can be used successfully to store and distribute high purity water. Figure 8-2 through Figure 8-12 present simplified schematic diagrams of each configuration.

1. Batched Tank Recirculating System (Figure 8-2)

This system is used where QA release is required on the water before it goes into the process. One batch tank supplies water to the process, while the other is filled and tested for QA release (traditionally due to unreliable means of water production). This is a cumbersome system to operate and is usually limited to smaller systems. The disadvantages are the high capital and operating costs. In-line conductivity and TOC measurements can provide nearly the same degree of assurance for less money.

2. Branched/One Way with Limited Points of Use (Figure 8-3)

This configuration is sometimes used where capital is tight, the system is small, and microbiological quality is of lesser concern. It is also useful where frequent flushing or sanitization of the piping is possible. It is a good application where water use is continuous. It is less advantageous where water use is sporadic, as the line stays stagnant when not in use. A program must be set up to flush (e.g., daily) and sanitize the loop to maintain microbial contamination within acceptable limits.

3. Parallel Loops, Single Tank (Figure 8-4)

This configuration is a combination of any of the loop distribution schemes off one storage tank. Parallel loops are very common and are most advantageous where multiple temperatures are required, or where the area served is so large that a single loop becomes cost prohibitive or hydraulically impractical. The major concern is to balance the various loops to maintain proper pressure and flow. This is accomplished either by using pressure control valves, or by providing a separate pump for each loop.

Commentary | 解說

8.3.4 系統架構實例（1-3）

1. 批次式循環系統

歷史性設計 適用於產水設備不可靠需要 QA 逐批放行的場景。現在有了線上 conductivity 和 TOC，這種設計已經很少見。

優點：水在使用前經過完整測試

缺點：高資本成本、操作複雜、需要至少兩個槽

2. 分支/單向式

高風險 最便宜但微生物控制最困難。停用時管線水滯留不動，必須建立每日沖洗和定期消毒 SOP。

適用：預算緊、系統小、持續用水、可頻繁消毒

不適用：間歇用水（管線會長時間滯留）

3. 平行迴路/單槽

常見設計 最大優勢是可以從同一個槽供應不同溫度的水。例如：一個熱迴路 + 一個冷卻再加熱迴路。

關鍵挑戰：各迴路之間的壓力和流量平衡。解決方式：壓力控制閥或各迴路獨立泵浦。

設計重點

ISPE 在此處強調 in-line conductivity 和 TOC 的價值 — 這些線上監測技術讓批次式系統的必要性大幅降低。這也呼應了前面「風險導向設計」的理念：用最合理的成本達到最大的品質保障。

Original Text

4. Hot Storage, Hot Distribution (Figure 8-5)

This is the configuration of choice when all use points require hot (greater than 65°C) water. Temperature is maintained in the storage tank by steam supplied to the tank jacket or alternatively by a heat exchanger on the circulating loop. Water is generally returned to the top of the tank through a spray ball to ensure that the entire top surface is wetted. This system provides excellent microbial control and is simple to operate. Tank and loop sanitization is required less frequently, or not at all, if a temperature of 80°C is maintained. This type of system is universally accepted by regulatory agencies.

Areas of concern include protecting workers from scalding, cavitation in the circulation pump, moisture condensation on the vent filter, and the formation of rouge. Scalding is minimized by operating at lower temperature (65°C) or by proper training and personal protective gear. Cavitation is avoided by accounting for the high vapor pressure of hot water in the NPSH calculations. Condensation is prevented by positioning the hydrophobic vent filter for good drainage and by heating the filter with either a low pressure steam jacket or electric tracing. Rouge formation is controlled by passivation and by operating at a lower temperature. It can be eliminated by using non-metallic or lined components.

5. Ambient Storage, Ambient Distribution (Figure 8-6 & 8-7)

This system is most advantageous when the water is generated at ambient temperature, will be used only at ambient temperature, and there is adequate time for sanitization.

Since the water is stored at ambient temperature with no disinfectant, microbial control is not as good as in hot storage system configurations. However, good microbial control is possible provided sanitization is conducted on a frequent basis. Frequent sanitization is usually accomplished by allowing the water level in the storage tank to drop through use, then heating the remaining contents, and circulating through the loop for a set amount of time.

The capital and operating costs of this system are minimal. Another advantage is that it can provide high flow rates of ambient pharmaceutical water, without need for complex points of use heat exchangers. Its major disadvantage is the time required to sanitize.

Many pharmaceutical water users have found that storing and distributing water at ambient temperatures with periodic sanitization, (utilizing either clean steam or heating to 80°C for microbial control) to be safe and cost effective. The ambient system can also be effectively operated with an ozonated storage and a periodically ozonated loop. Levels of 0.02ppm to 0.2ppm of ozone protect the water from microbial recontamination. Ozone needs to be completely removed from process water prior to usage, using UV radiation. One advantage of ozonation or chemical sanitization, is that these methods allow the use of plastics as a material of construction (popular in Europe for purified water systems).

Commentary | 解說

系統架構實例（4-5）

4. 熱儲存 + 熱配送 — 黃金標準

法規首選 這是最被法規機關認可的系統架構 — 80°C 持續循環，微生物控制最優，操作最簡單。如果你不確定選哪種，選這個就對了。

熱系統四大風險與對策

風險對策
人員燙傷降溫至 65°C、防護裝備、教育訓練
泵浦氣蝕正確計算 NPSH（考慮高溫水蒸氣壓）
呼吸過濾器結露加熱夾套或電伴熱，確保排水順暢
Rouge 形成鈍化處理、降低溫度、使用非金屬材料

5. 常溫儲存 + 常溫配送

成本最低 適合水在常溫產出且使用的場景。成本最低但需要頻繁消毒。

常溫系統消毒策略

  • 熱水消毒：讓水位自然下降（用掉一部分水），然後加熱剩餘的水至 80°C 循環消毒。好處是減少加熱能源。
  • 臭氧消毒（Figure 8-7）：0.02-0.2ppm 臭氧持續保護。使用前必須用 UV 完全去除臭氧。歐洲 PW 系統較常見。
  • 化學消毒：允許使用塑膠材料（PVDF、PP），降低成本。

比喻：游泳池 vs. 溫泉

熱系統就像溫泉 — 高溫本身就是消毒手段。常溫系統就像游泳池 — 需要額外加入消毒劑（臭氧或化學品）才能維持水質。選哪種取決於你的使用情境和預算。

Original Text

6. Hot Storage, Cool and Reheat (Figure 8-8)

This system is most advantageous when the water is generated hot, tight microbial control is required, and there is little time for sanitization. It provides excellent microbial control and is easily sanitized. It requires less capital than point of use exchangers, if there are multiple low temperature use points.
