a) Although non-ion specific, conductivity is a valuable tool for measuring the total ionic quality of water and is a critical parameter for many high purity water systems. Conductivity limits for Purified Water and WFI are specified in the USP.

b) On-Line conductivity instrumentation is frequently used to monitor and control the performance of many types of purification equipment and to continuously monitor the quality of pharmaceutical waters. On-line conductivity instrumentation may also be used for final quality assurance testing, thus eliminating the need for periodic laboratory analysis of water samples.

c) Temperature has a profound impact upon conductivity measurement. To eliminate this temperature dependence, most instruments include a temperature sensor in the conductivity probe and one or more algorithms to correct the actual measurement to a standard temperature. However, due to the inaccuracy inherent in temperature compensation algorithms, compensated conductivity measurements are not suitable for critical quality assurance testing of USP purified water and WFI. When In-Line conductivity measurements are used for final quality assurance testing of USP purified water and WFI, a non-compensated conductivity value and the water temperature must be measured as required by the USP.

Compensated conductivity values used strictly for process control and monitoring are not subject to USP requirements.

d) To operate properly, conductivity sensors must be installed such that there is continuous water flow through the sensor and air bubbles or solids cannot become trapped inside the electrodes. Air bubbles will result in lower-than-expected conductivity readings while solids can impact the conductivity in either direction. Clean steam must be condensed prior to conductivity measurement.

e) Conductivity meters may be used throughout a pharmaceutical water system to monitor and control purification processes or to monitor pharmaceutical water quality. Some examples are:

  • Feed water monitoring can detect seasonal or unanticipated quality changes that could impact pretreatment equipment operation.
  • RO influent and effluent monitoring allows calculation and trending of percentage rejection. Changes in percentage rejection may be a sign of membrane failure, scaling or fouling, seal failure, improper pH, inadequate feed pressure, or too high a recovery rate.
  • Deionizer effluent or in-bed monitoring detects, or predicts, resin exhaustion and allows automatic initiation of regeneration cycles.
  • Final treatment step monitoring verifies acceptable quality prior to delivery to a storage tank. In addition, conductivity meters are often installed in the return piping of distribution loops downstream of the final point of use. Many systems include provisions for automatic diversion to drain or recirculation back through purification equipment when water quality entering the tank is outside the acceptable range.

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念解析 Key Concepts

導電度 -- 水系統的「體溫計」：

導電度是製藥水系統中最重要的單一參數。它雖然無法辨識具體是哪種離子（non-ion specific），但能快速反映水的整體離子品質。就像體溫計無法告訴你是哪個器官發炎，但溫度升高就知道身體出了問題。

溫度補償的關鍵區分：

  • 補償值 (Compensated)：儀器自動將讀值修正到 25°C 的等效值。適用於製程監控，方便趨勢比較
  • 非補償值 (Non-compensated)：實際溫度下的原始導電度讀值。USP 要求最終品質測試必須使用此值 + 水溫，對照 USP 的溫度-導電度表

為什麼 USP 不接受補償值？因為所有溫度補償演算法都有固有誤差，在高純水的微小導電度範圍內，這個誤差可能導致合格/不合格的誤判。

重點提示 Key Notes

導電度監測點的佈局策略：

監測位置監測目的Critical?
原水進水偵測季節性水質變化通常 Non-Critical
RO 進出水計算脫鹽率、偵測膜故障視系統設計而定
DI 出水 / 床層內預測樹脂耗竭視系統設計而定
最終處理步驟後進入儲槽前的品質確認通常 Critical
迴路末端回水持續監控分配水質通常 Critical

常見錯誤 Common Mistakes

氣泡造成的假性合格：空氣泡會導致導電度讀值偏低（因為空氣不導電）。這意味著系統可能顯示「合格」，但實際水質已經不合格。這是一個隱蔽的風險，特別在以下情況需要注意：

  • 泵浦出口附近（cavitation 產生氣泡）
  • 溫度劇烈變化的位置（flashing）
  • 管路變徑或方向急轉處（turbulence）

9.3.3.2 Total Organic Carbon (TOC)

原文內容 Original Text

a) Total Organic Carbon (TOC) is a measure of the carbon dissolved in water in the form of organic compounds. It is a valuable tool for measuring the aggregate level of organic impurities in pharmaceutical water systems. A TOC test with a nominal limit of 500 ppb for USP Purified Water and WFI is a required test in the USP.

b) TOC meters are relatively sophisticated analytical instruments. The USP provides guidance on how to qualify an instrument and how to interpret the instrument results.

c) In addition to "continuous" monitoring of equipment performance and pharmaceutical water quality, On-Line TOC meters may be used for final quality assurance testing, thus eliminating the need for periodic laboratory analysis. When used for critical assurance testing of USP purified water and WFI, instrument precision, system suitability, test methodology and calibration procedures must meet USP requirements. Instruments used strictly for process control and monitoring are not subject to USP requirements.

d) TOC is often monitored at several locations in a pharmaceutical water system. Some examples are:

  • Feed water monitoring can detect seasonal or unanticipated quality changes that could impact pretreatment equipment operation or the potential for resin or membrane fouling.
  • Downstream of carbon filters, organic scavengers, RO units, and UV lights can verify proper equipment operation and provide advance warning of bed exhaustion, compromised membranes, or the need for lamp replacement.
  • Final treatment step monitoring of pharmaceutical water verifies acceptable quality prior to delivery to a storage tank. TOC meters are often installed in the return piping of distribution loops downstream of the final point of use. Many systems include provisions for automatic diversion to drain or recirculation back through purification equipment when water quality is outside the acceptable range.

e) There has been much interest in the possible use of TOC analyzers to indicate endotoxin contamination. While this type of contamination will lead to higher TOC levels, there is no quantitative correlation to TOC levels. TOC results cannot substitute for microbial or endotoxin testing.

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念解析 Key Concepts

TOC -- 有機物的「總帳」：

導電度量測的是離子（無機物），TOC 量測的是有機碳。兩者互補，構成水質監控的兩大支柱。USP 規定 PW 和 WFI 的 TOC 限值為 500 ppb (0.5 mg/L)，這是取代舊版「可氧化物質」(Oxidizable Substances) 濕化學測試的現代方法。

兩種用途的差異化要求：

  • 製程監控用：不受 USP 儀器要求約束，可用較簡單的設備
  • 最終品質測試用：必須符合 USP 對儀器精密度、系統適用性、測試方法和校正程序的要求

重點提示 Key Notes

TOC 與內毒素的關係 -- 一個常見的誤解：

業界一直有人希望用 TOC 來「間接」監測內毒素。ISPE 的立場很明確：TOC 不能替代內毒素測試。

  • 內毒素是脂多醣 (lipopolysaccharide)，確實含有碳，會貢獻 TOC
  • 但 TOC 讀值包含所有有機碳來源（清潔劑殘留、樹脂溶出物、管路材質等），無法區分哪些是內毒素貢獻的
  • 沒有可靠的定量關聯式可以從 TOC 值推算內毒素濃度

結論：TOC 合格 ≠ 內毒素合格。兩個測試必須獨立執行。

CDMO 應用場景 Application

TOC 自動排放 (Auto-dump) 策略：

ISPE 提到許多系統在水質超標時會自動排放到 drain 或回流到純化設備。在 CDMO 環境中，這個功能的設計需要特別考慮：

  • 自動排放的觸發值 (setpoint) 應該在 action limit 之前，通常設在 alert limit
  • 排放事件必須自動記錄，包含時間戳、持續時間、觸發值
  • 頻繁的自動排放可能暗示系統有更深層的問題，需要根因調查

9.3.3.3 pH

原文內容 Original Text

a) pH measurement is relatively straightforward for high conductivity water. Reliable results can generally be obtained using pH indicators or laboratory, field, or On-Line pH meters.

b) Accurate pH measurement is difficult in many pharmaceutical waters due to the low conductivity. Low conductivity water is susceptible to pH swings due to contaminants introduced from the air, sample containers, and test equipment, as well as instrument difficulties associated with measuring low ionic strength solutions.

c) Common locations for On-Line pH measurement and control include:

  • Upstream of cellulose acetate RO membranes, where acid is injected to minimize membrane hydrolysis
  • Upstream of a degasifier, where acid is injected to increase CO2 removal

d) While pH limits for purified water and WFI are no longer specified in the USP, On-Line pH meters are rarely used for process control or for final quality assurance testing of pharmaceutical waters for several reasons:

  • Conductivity is a more sensitive measurement of overall ionic quality since changes in pH reflect logarithmic changes in water quality
  • A pH sensor's reference electrode contains a buffer solution that may leak through the reference electrode into the water being measured. To prevent contamination of the pharmaceutical water system, a pH sensor is installed in a side stream that flows to drain.
  • pH meters require frequent (daily in some cases) calibration with standard buffer solutions

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念解析 Key Concepts

為什麼高純水的 pH 這麼難測？

高純水的導電度極低（< 1.3 μS/cm），這意味著水中幾乎沒有離子。在這種條件下：

  • 空氣中的 CO2 溶入水中形成碳酸，瞬間改變 pH
  • 取樣容器表面的微量溶出物就能影響讀值
  • pH 電極的參考液 (buffer) 可能洩漏到水中，既影響讀值又污染系統

導電度 vs pH 的靈敏度比較：pH 是對數尺度，pH 從 7.0 變到 6.0 代表 H+ 濃度增加了 10 倍。但導電度是線性的，能更敏感地反映微小的離子變化。這就是為什麼 USP 已經移除了 PW/WFI 的 pH 規格，改用導電度作為主要品質指標。

重點提示 Key Notes

pH 探頭的污染風險：ISPE 特別提到，pH 感測器的參考電極含有緩衝液，可能洩漏到水中。這不是理論風險 -- 在實際案例中，pH 探頭的 KCl 緩衝液洩漏曾導致整個配液批次的導電度超標。因此：

  • pH 探頭永遠安裝在旁路 (side stream)，排放到 drain
  • 不可將旁路水回流到主系統
  • 在 PW/WFI 分配迴路中，幾乎不安裝線上 pH 計

9.3.3.4 Ozone

原文內容 Original Text

a) Dissolved ozone levels should be monitored in storage and distribution systems that utilize ozone for microbial control. Ozone levels can be determined periodically in the laboratory using several wet chemistry methods, or continuously using an On-Line analyzer.

b) For effective and safe system operation, ozone levels should be monitored at the following locations:

  • At the storage tank discharge to control operation of the ozone generator
  • Downstream of the UV light to ensure ozone destruction prior to water use
  • In loop return piping to ensure proper ozone levels are maintained during sanitization

c) Since Oxidation Reduction Potential (ORP) analyzers are nonspecific and unable to differentiate ozone from other oxidants, ORP analyzers should not be used for controlling ozone levels in pharmaceutical water systems.

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念解析 Key Concepts

臭氧監測的三個關鍵位置：

  • 儲槽出口：控制臭氧產生器的啟停，確保槽內維持足夠的殘餘臭氧濃度
  • UV 燈下游：確認臭氧已被完全破壞。殘餘臭氧進入製程會氧化藥品成分，造成品質問題
  • 迴路回水管：確認整個迴路的臭氧消毒效果維持在有效濃度

重點提示 Key Notes

ORP 不能替代臭氧專用分析儀！

ORP（氧化還原電位）看似是一個方便的替代方案，但它無法區分臭氧和其他氧化劑（如氯、過氧化氫）。在製藥水系統中，如果原水中含有微量餘氯，ORP 讀值會「灌水」，讓你誤以為臭氧濃度足夠。ISPE 明確禁止用 ORP 來控制臭氧。

9.3.3.5 - 9.3.3.8 Flow, Temperature, Pressure, Level

原文內容 Original Text

9.3.3.5 Flow

A wide variety of flow meters may be used in the feed water and pretreatment portion of a pharmaceutical water system including magnetic flow meters, mass flow meters, vortex shedding meters, and ultrasonic meters. All meters should be installed according to the manufacturer's instructions, to ensure proper operation.

Water flow rate (or velocity) may help to reduce microbial growth and maintain temperature within hot or cold systems. It is commonly verified upon startup, but not continuously monitored. Flow rate may vary. It may be monitored for information only.

9.3.3.6 Temperature

Temperature is often monitored and/or controlled at various locations to ensure optimum equipment operation and/or for microbial control. Temperature interlocks may be used to prevent damage to membranes, resins, or equipment if water temperatures drift outside allowable ranges.

In distribution systems where temperature is controlled or where heat sanitization is used, temperature is considered critical to ensure proper system operation or effective sanitization.

9.3.3.7 Pressure

Pressure may be monitored and controlled throughout the purification process to ensure optimum equipment operation. Monitoring differential pressure across filters indicates when backwashing or element replacement is needed. Differential pressure measurement across resin beds is useful in detecting resin fouling and poor flow distribution. Monitoring RO feed, interstage, permeate, and concentrate pressures provides early warning of membrane fouling and scaling. Back pressure control in distribution systems may be critical, if minimum pressures are required at points of use.

Pressure is not normally considered a critical parameter, however, the system should maintain positive pressure at all times. It may typically be monitored for information only.

9.3.3.8 Level

Various types of level measurement are used in the feed water and pretreatment portion of a pharmaceutical water system, including simple float switches, ultrasonic sensors, capacitance sensors, and differential pressure transmitters. The stub from the tank must be kept as short as possible to minimize deadlegs. Calibration of this type of transmitter is time consuming, since it requires filling the tank to verify proper operation. Tank nozzles with integral valves minimize deadlegs and allow calibration while the tank is in service.

Tank level may be monitored to control the supply of water into a tank and for control and protection of downstream pumps. In some instances, level may not normally be considered a critical parameter and may be monitored for information only. In these cases, it is usually not validated.

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念解析 Key Concepts

四大物理參數的 Critical 分級：

參數通常是否 Critical何時變 Critical
Flow（流量）Non-Critical當流速直接用於微生物控制策略（如維持湍流）時
Temperature（溫度）視情況熱水消毒系統中 = Critical；常溫系統 = 通常 Non-Critical
Pressure（壓力）通常 Non-Critical當正壓維持是防止外部污染的策略時（如防止空氣回流）
Level（液位）通常 Non-Critical極少被視為 Critical

注意 ISPE 的措辭：「may not be considered critical」、「monitored for information only」。這代表這些參數的 Critical/Non-Critical 分類不是絕對的，取決於你的系統設計和微生物控制策略。

比喻說明 Analogy

想像你在管理一棟大樓的消防系統：

  • 流量 (Flow) 就像消防栓的出水量 -- 平時不需要一直量，但消防演練時必須確認
  • 溫度 (Temperature) 就像灑水系統的防凍設計 -- 在寒冷地區是 critical，在熱帶就不是
  • 壓力 (Pressure) 就像水管的水壓 -- 只要有水就好，但如果壓力歸零，消防栓就無法使用
  • 液位 (Level) 就像水塔的水位 -- 只要不空、不溢就好，精確值不那麼重要

重點提示 Key Notes

正壓 (Positive Pressure) 的重要性：ISPE 雖然說壓力通常不是 critical parameter，但特別強調系統必須「隨時維持正壓」。為什麼？

  • 負壓會導致外部空氣（及其中的微生物）被吸入系統
  • 密封不完美的接頭、墊片在負壓時可能成為微生物入侵的通道
  • 特別是在 POU（使用點）取水後，如果沒有適當的背壓控制，管路末端可能瞬間出現負壓

液位校正的實務挑戰 Practical Tip

ISPE 提到一個很實際的問題：差壓式液位計的校正需要把儲槽填滿水來驗證，非常耗時。解決方案：

  • 選擇帶有整合閥門 (integral valve) 的儲槽接嘴，可以在儲槽運行中進行校正
  • 接管 (stub) 要盡量短，減少 deadleg
  • 超音波或電容式液位計通常比差壓式更容易校正
9.4 Design Conditions versus Operating Range

原文內容 Original Text

The control system may recognize the distinction between design conditions and operating ranges, and the impact this distinction has upon validation and facility operation. These criteria are defined as:

  • Design Condition: the specified range, or accuracy, of a controlled variable used by the designer as a basis to determine the performance requirements for an engineered system.
  • Allowable Operating Range: the range of validated critical parameters within which acceptable water product can be produced.
  • Normal Operating Range: a range that may be selected as the desired acceptable values for a parameter during normal operations. This range must be within the Allowable Operating Range.

a) While it is desirable that a facility should meet all stated design conditions, the acceptability of the water system for operation from a cGMP standpoint depends on operating within the Allowable Operating Ranges.

b) Normal Operating Ranges cannot exceed the Allowable Operating Range for the product water. Design condition selection should reflect Good Engineering Practice.

c) It may be desirable to apply the concept of Alert and Action points along with Normal Operating Range. Alert levels are based on normal operating experience and are used to initiate investigations or corrective measures, before reaching an Action level. Action levels are defined as the level at which some corrective action must be taken to avoid jeopardizing water quality.

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念解析 Key Concepts

三層架構的巢狀關係：

Design Condition（設計條件）-- 最寬
↓
Allowable Operating Range（允許操作範圍）-- 驗證確認
↓
Normal Operating Range（正常操作範圍）-- 最窄

關鍵區別：

  • Design Condition：工程師設計時的依據，包含安全餘裕 (safety margin)。設備「能夠」運作的範圍
  • Allowable Operating Range：經過驗證證實在此範圍內可以生產合格水的範圍。這是 cGMP 合規的判定基準
  • Normal Operating Range：日常操作的目標範圍，必須在 Allowable Range 之內

重點提示 Key Notes

Alert vs Action Level 的運用：

  • Alert Level（警戒值）：基於正常操作經驗設定。觸發時啟動調查，但不一定要停止生產。相當於「黃燈」
  • Action Level（行動值）：必須採取矯正措施。可能需要停止供水、排放至 drain、或回流再處理。相當於「紅燈」

在實務中，Alert/Action Level 的設定應基於 Phase 1-3 驗證期間收集的歷史數據，而非憑感覺。常見的做法是用統計方法（如 mean + 2σ 作為 Alert，mean + 3σ 作為 Action），但必須確保 Action Level 仍在 Allowable Operating Range 之內。

練習思考 Practice Questions

  1. 你的儲槽溫度 Design Condition 是 80±5°C，Allowable Range 是 75-85°C，Normal Range 是 78-82°C。如果某天溫度跌到 74°C，這代表什麼？你應該怎麼做？
  2. 如果 Alert Level 和 Action Level 設定得太接近，會造成什麼操作問題？
  3. 為什麼 ISPE 說「從 cGMP 角度，接受標準是 Allowable Operating Range」而不是 Design Condition？
9.5 Instrumentation Spikes

原文內容 Original Text

"Spikes" may be experienced in the measurement of some parameters. These excursions may be the result of the measurement technique or sensor and may not be representative of the actual parameter value. If a spike occurs in a system with a significant physical lag or mass, the rapid changes in a parameter as evidenced by spikes may be physically impossible and consequently can be treated as instrumentation spikes.

In other cases, it may be decided to treat these spikes as Alert Level deviations based upon their frequency and duration even though their magnitude may exceed the Action level.

A procedure for defining and handling spikes should be developed in conjunction with Quality Assurance based on the specific water system.

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念解析 Key Concepts

Spike（尖峰）的判定邏輯：

ISPE 提供了一個很實用的物理邏輯判定法：

  • 如果系統有很大的物理慣性（如 10,000L 儲槽），溫度不可能在 1 秒內從 80°C 跳到 60°C
  • 如果數據上出現這種「物理上不可能」的突變，就可以判定為儀器尖峰，而非真正的製程偏移
  • 但如果是在流通量小、反應快的管路上（如 POU 出水口的導電度），同樣的突變可能是真實的

重點提示 Key Notes

Spike 處理的三個關鍵要求：

  1. 必須有書面程序 (SOP)：定義什麼是 spike、判定標準、處理流程。不能事後才「解釋」
  2. 必須與 QA 共同制定：這不是純工程決策，需要品質保證部門的認可
  3. 基於特定系統設計：每個水系統的物理特性不同，spike 的判定標準也不同

Data Integrity 的隱患：Spike 處理是審計人員最關注的領域之一。如果你的系統經常出現「被歸類為 spike」的偏移，但又沒有根因調查，會被質疑是否在用 spike classification 來掩蓋真正的品質問題。

比喻說明 Analogy

想像你在量體溫。你把耳溫槍放進耳朵量了一次：39.5°C。再量一次：36.8°C。第三次：36.7°C。那第一次的 39.5°C 很可能是「spike」-- 也許探頭沒有完全對準鼓膜。但如果你連續三次都量到 38.5°C 以上，那就是真的發燒了。製藥水系統也一樣：偶發的、物理上不合理的讀值可以視為 spike，但持續性的偏移必須認真對待。

21 CFR Part 11 關聯 Regulatory Connection

如果你的控制系統自動過濾或標記 spike 數據，這涉及電子記錄的完整性：

  • 原始數據（包含 spike）必須保留，不得刪除
  • 任何數據的重新分類（從 Action 改為 Spike）都必須有審計追蹤 (audit trail)
  • 過濾演算法的邏輯必須經過驗證
  • 這是 Data Integrity 審計中常被查核的項目
9.6 Control Systems

9.6.1 Level of Automation

原文內容 Original Text

Selection of a control strategy for a pharmaceutical water system should consider feed water quality and reliability, the complexity of the purification and/or distribution system, labor costs, personnel skill levels and capabilities, and documentation and reporting requirements. Options for control include:

a) Local instrumentation with manual control: In this option, a combination of instrumentation, periodic samples, and visual examination is used to monitor critical process parameters. Data is collected and recorded manually, and analysis and trending capabilities are limited. Excursions of critical parameters outside acceptable ranges typically trigger local alarms to reduce the risk of unacceptable water quality. Satisfactory manual operation requires significant human intervention. This requires detailed operating procedures and conscientious documentation of critical quality parameters. This option has the lowest installed cost, but is very labor intensive and may be subject to human error.

b) Semi-automatic control: These systems use local operator control panels, relay logic control, local chart recorders and printers, and some manual data collection to monitor and control the water system. These systems are less labor intensive over the manual systems, but are still labor intensive due to the manual data collection and monitoring required to control the process.

c) Automatic control: Automated systems use a computer (PLC or DCS), or computers, to control the pharmaceutical water system. The computer system utilizes appropriate process monitoring instrumentation (conductivity probes, flow meters, etc.) to gather data and make appropriate adjustments to the system automatically. As water generation systems become more sophisticated, relying on human intervention to control and monitor the water system becomes more difficult and labor intensive. An automated system requires less operator involvement, but requires a more highly trained maintenance and engineering support staff.

d) Fully integrated systems: These systems include a fully automated system and a wide area network connected to other computer systems in the building or site. These systems allow for central site monitoring, automatic electronic data collection, centralized alarm monitoring with automatic recording, response, and report generation.

Additional information on control system design is available in the Good Automated Manufacturing Practice (GAMP) Guide and in various guidelines by the Instrument Society of America (ISA).

Whichever level of automation is selected, the validation effort should verify operation of the complete system, including vendor-supplied sub-systems.

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念解析 Key Concepts

四級自動化的比較：

等級特點優點缺點
Level 1
Manual		 手動監控、手動記錄 安裝成本最低 人力密集、人為錯誤風險高
Level 2
Semi-Auto		 本地控制面板、圖表記錄器 比手動省力 仍需大量手動數據收集
Level 3
Auto		 PLC/DCS 自動控制 操作員介入少、數據完整 需要高技能維護人員
Level 4
Integrated		 全廠網路整合、中央監控 集中管理、自動報表 最高安裝成本、網路安全風險

重點提示 Key Notes

現代 CDMO 的選擇：在 2020 年代，幾乎所有新建的製藥水系統都採用 Level 3 或 Level 4。原因：

  • Data Integrity 要求：手動記錄的數據完整性風險太高，自動化電子記錄符合 21 CFR Part 11 的期望
  • 審計期望提升：客戶和法規機構期望看到完整的電子趨勢圖、自動告警記錄、audit trail
  • 複雜度增加：現代水系統整合了多種技術（RO、EDI、UV、臭氧等），人工操控已不切實際
  • 人力成本：24/7 的手動監控成本遠高於自動化系統的維護成本

CDMO 應用場景 Application

Level 4 整合系統的實際價值：

在 CDMO 環境中，Level 4 的全廠整合有額外的優勢：

  • 水系統數據可以自動關聯到批次記錄 (batch record)，證明生產期間的水質合規
  • 遠端監控讓工程團隊可以在非上班時間回應告警
  • 集中的報表生成功能大幅減少 Annual Product Review 和客戶審計的準備時間

但要注意：整合度越高，21 CFR Part 11 和 GAMP 5 的驗證範圍也越大。

9.6.2 Control System Software

原文內容 Original Text

The software/control system may be used to measure, monitor, control, or record critical process parameters. Programming and design standards, especially concerning operator interface, control techniques, alarm handling, and interlock processing should be applied during the development, validation and maintenance phases of the project. The control system software consists of:

a) Firmware, Operating System and Application Software: This is software permanently loaded into memory that may or may not be accessible to the user. While the functions performed by the control system may be divided between critical and non-critical functions, it is impossible to divide or isolate the firmware, operating system, application software, and associated hardware functions. Therefore, if some of the functions of a control system are considered critical, all of the above software is considered critical, and should be validated.

b) User Configurable Software: The functions of the user configurable software may be defined as critical or non-critical. The critical functions or modules require enhanced documentation, including validation. In some cases, it may be impossible to divide or isolate software adequately. In such cases, if some of the functions are critical, it may be necessary to validate all the software.

The type of process control required is often the determining factor in the type of software needed, and software requirements often define the type of system selected. Major considerations are:

  • Number of I/O points
  • Mathematical or statistical functions required
  • Reporting features required (particularly if the control system is to be further integrated into higher systems)
  • Whether or not advanced control techniques are required (e.g., neural nets; fuzzy logic controllers; adaptive gain; dead-time compensation)

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念解析 Key Concepts

軟體驗證的「連帶責任」原則：

ISPE 提出一個重要的驗證原則：如果控制系統的任何功能被視為 critical，則底層的 firmware、OS、application software 都必須被視為 critical 並進行驗證。為什麼？

  • Firmware bug 可能影響所有上層功能
  • OS 崩潰會導致所有 critical 和 non-critical 功能同時失效
  • 無法在軟體層面完全隔離 critical 和 non-critical 的執行路徑

這個原則直接影響驗證的範圍和成本 -- 你不能只驗證「用到的功能」，而必須驗證整個平台。

重點提示 Key Notes

GAMP 5 分類與水系統控制軟體：

GAMP Category描述水系統中的例子
Cat 1基礎設施軟體PLC 的 firmware、SCADA 的 OS
Cat 3非客製化軟體SCADA 平台（如 Siemens WinCC）
Cat 4可配置軟體PLC 程式、HMI 畫面配置
Cat 5客製化軟體自寫的資料分析模組

Category 越高，驗證要求越嚴格。現代趨勢是盡量使用 Cat 3-4（現成平台 + 配置），減少 Cat 5（客製化開發），以降低驗證負擔。

21 CFR Part 11 關聯 Regulatory Connection

控制系統軟體與電子記錄法規：

當控制系統用於記錄 critical process parameters 時，21 CFR Part 11 的要求適用：

  • Audit Trail：對任何設定值、配置、alarm setpoint 的更改都必須有時間戳和操作者身份記錄
  • Access Control：不同層級的人員（操作員、工程師、管理者）有不同的權限
  • Electronic Signatures：關鍵操作（如修改 alarm limit）可能需要電子簽名
  • Data Backup：所有電子記錄必須定期備份，並可在需要時完整恢復

9.6.3 Control Hardware and Operation Interface

原文內容 Original Text

a) Critical software requires enhanced documentation and should be designed and tested in accordance with the Lifecycle Methodology.

b) The water system, field instruments and control requirements all affect control hardware selection. Plant standards, or a large installed base of a particular system may drive the selection.

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念解析 Key Concepts

Lifecycle Methodology（生命週期方法）：

ISPE 引用的 Lifecycle Methodology 即後來 GAMP 5 所定義的 V-Model：

  • 左側（開發）：User Requirements → Functional Spec → Design Spec → Code/Config
  • 右側（驗證）：Unit Test → Integration Test → OQ → PQ
  • 每一層的開發文件都對應一層驗證活動

硬體選擇的實務考量：ISPE 提到「工廠標準」和「現有裝機量」會影響選型。在 CDMO 中這特別重要 -- 如果全廠都用 Siemens PLC，水系統也應該統一，以便維護團隊能共用技能和備品。

練習思考 Practice Questions

  1. 你的工廠計劃新建一套水系統，IT 部門建議使用最新的物聯網 (IoT) 平台來取代傳統 SCADA。從 GMP 驗證的角度，你會如何評估這個建議？
  2. 如果供應商交付的 PLC 程式中包含 50 個功能塊，其中只有 10 個與 critical parameters 有關，你是否可以只驗證這 10 個？為什麼？
  3. 在選擇水系統的 HMI（人機介面）時，哪些 21 CFR Part 11 的要求會影響你的設計？

本章總結 Chapter Summary

  • Critical vs Non-Critical 的核心邏輯：影響最終水質的參數 = Critical Parameter，對應儀器需要 Qualification；其餘按 GEP Commissioning 即可。文件和現場標示必須清楚區分兩者。
  • 導電度是最重要的線上監測參數：USP 最終品質測試必須使用非溫度補償值 + 水溫，對照 USP 表格判定。溫度補償值僅適用於製程監控。氣泡是導電度測量的最大隱患。
  • TOC 與導電度互補：TOC 量測有機碳，導電度量測離子。兩者共同構成水質監控的完整圖像。TOC 不能替代內毒素測試。
  • pH 在高純水系統中的角色有限：因為靈敏度不如導電度、探頭有污染風險、需頻繁校正，USP 已移除 pH 規格。主要用於前處理段的酸鹼調節。
  • Design Condition ⊃ Allowable Operating Range ⊃ Normal Operating Range：cGMP 合規的判定基準是 Allowable Operating Range。Alert/Action Level 應基於歷史數據的統計分析設定。
  • Spike 的處理需要書面程序：判定標準基於物理可能性，必須與 QA 共同制定。原始數據（含 spike）不可刪除，重新分類需有 audit trail。
  • 現代水系統以 Level 3-4 自動化為主流：PLC/DCS 自動控制配合 SCADA 集中監控。控制軟體的驗證遵循「連帶責任」原則 -- 任何 critical 功能的存在，使整個軟體平台都需要驗證。21 CFR Part 11 要求適用於所有電子記錄功能。

ISPE Baseline® Guide Volume 4: Water and Steam Systems

Chapter 9: Instrumentation and Control | p147-p156

SterileGMP Knowledge Hub — Bilingual Educational Reference

10

Chapter 10: Commissioning & Qualification

試車與確效認證 | p157-p163

ISPE Baseline® Guide Volume 4: Water and Steam Systems | p157-p163

本章學習目標

  • 理解水系統確效（Qualification）文件的特殊要求，以及為何水系統需要「超越一般設備」的額外關注
  • 掌握三階段取樣計畫（Phase 1 / 2 / 3）的目的、持續時間與取樣策略差異
  • 理解化學與微生物接受標準的建立邏輯，包括 Alert / Action Limit 的概念
  • 認識確效報告的撰寫時機與審批流程（中期報告 vs. 總結報告）
  • 掌握變更管制與再確效（Requalification）的觸發機制
  • 建立與 ISPE Vol.5 C&Q 框架的交叉參照概念

本章目錄 Chapter Contents

10.2 文件要求 10.3 取樣計畫 10.3.1 Phase 1 10.3.2 Phase 2 10.3.3 Phase 3 10.4 接受標準 10.5 確效報告 10.6 變更管制
10.2 System Qualification Documentation 系統確效文件

原文內容 Original Text

The system qualification documentation for water systems requires additional emphasis on certain areas. The documentation set should include:

  • A schematic drawing of the system (P&ID)
  • Written system specifications
  • Detailed design drawings
  • Vendor manuals and drawings
  • Field inspection and test reports
  • System qualification test results

Because of their critical impact on pharmaceutical production, water systems require additional emphasis on certain sections of this documentation. Specific design requirements for water and steam systems are contained within the body of this Guide. When compiling documentation related to water systems, particular attention should be paid to the following:

a) Schematic documentation may be enhanced by the inclusion of a system isometric diagram (or diagrams) indicating location and numbering of welds, relative elevations, slope of lines, and points of drainage.

b) The system specification should indicate performance criteria, as well as design parameters.

c) Field inspection and test reports should include cleaning and passivation procedure and record, weld parameter documentation and inspection reports, slope verification, and verification of the absence of "dead-legs".

d) System qualification tests may or may not be subject to a pre-approved protocol addressing qualification test requirements. In either case, test results should be reported in direct comparison to acceptance criteria derived from system design and operating specifications.

e) System qualification tests should include verification of all automated functions, specified temperature control, distribution system velocity, and initial water quality determination.

"Additional details regarding water system qualification may be found in the associated ISPE Baseline® Guide on Commissioning and Qualification."

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念：水系統文件為何需要「額外關注」

一般設備的確效文件通常是標準 IQ/OQ/PQ 套路，但水系統之所以特殊，是因為：

  • 直接接觸產品：水是最常用的製藥原料/清洗溶劑，對產品品質影響直接且廣泛
  • 持續運行系統：不像單一設備可以批次驗證，水系統 24/7 運作，品質必須時刻維持
  • 動態微生物風險：水系統的微生物負荷會隨時間、季節、使用率變化

因此，文件必須涵蓋更多工程細節（等角圖、焊接紀錄、坡度驗證等），不能只有「通過/不通過」的簡單記錄。

重點提示：等角圖（Isometric Diagram）的實務價值

原文特別提到 isometric diagram 要標示：

  • 焊接位置與編號 — 每一道焊接都可追溯
  • 相對高程 — 確認管路排水性
  • 管路坡度 — 防止積水造成微生物溫床
  • 排水點 — 確認系統可完全排空

在 CDMO 的稽查中，稽查員經常要求「帶我去看這道焊縫在哪裡」。如果等角圖與現場不一致，就是重大 observation。

交叉參照：ISPE Vol.5 C&Q 框架

原文最後提到「詳細內容請參考 ISPE Commissioning and Qualification Baseline Guide」，即 ISPE Vol.5。Vol.5 提供的框架包括：

  • 系統分類（Direct / Indirect / No Impact）— 決定確效深度
  • 風險評估驅動的測試 — 不是所有項目都需要 GMP 確效
  • Leveraging Commissioning — 利用試車數據作為確效證據

水系統在 Vol.5 框架中屬於 Direct Impact System，因此所有確效測試都需要完整的 GMP 文件支持。但 Vol.5 的核心訊息是：良好的 commissioning 可以大幅減少確效階段的重複工作。

比喻說明 Analogy

水系統的確效文件就像一棟大樓的「竣工檔案」。普通住宅可能只需要基本的電氣和消防檢查報告，但醫院（相當於水系統的關鍵等級）需要的文件遠遠更多：每一段管路的X光焊檢報告、空調系統的平衡報告、緊急電源的切換測試紀錄……因為一旦出問題，影響的是病患安全。

練習思考 Practice Questions

  1. 原文列出六類文件。如果你只能優先完成三類，你會選哪三個？為什麼？
  2. 原文說確效測試「may or may not be subject to a pre-approved protocol」。在你的公司，確效測試是否使用預核准方案？不使用的風險是什麼？
10.3 System Qualification Sampling Program 系統確效取樣計畫

原文內容 Original Text

The qualification of water systems is unique in that performance must be proven over an extended period of time, and is subject to variations in use rate and initial feed water quality. Therefore, the sampling program associated with pharmaceutical water systems validation is unique and specialized.

Extensive sampling is required to establish and confirm that the entire system will operate within specified operating ranges, to develop and evaluate the system operation and maintenance procedures, and to verify that the water produced and delivered to the points of use consistently meets the required quality specifications and acceptance criteria. This portion of the program is sometimes termed performance qualification.

Because of the critical impact that water has upon pharmaceutical quality, the sampling program and evaluation of results is usually subject to a pre-approved plan or protocol, with clearly defined acceptance criteria. Also included should be procedures to deal with deviations from specified parameters and analytical results.

The sampling program consists of three successive phases, each with a specific purpose and sampling scheme, as outlined below. The initial phase of the sampling program typically begins after the water system is shown to be fully operational, as demonstrated through integrated system testing in Operational Qualification.

The water generated during the various phases may be used for manufacturing as long as analytical results are acceptable. The intended applications and impact of water quality should be considered in determining how much data is required before use.

Table 10-1: Sampling Program

Phase Typical Duration Primary Objectives
1 2-4 weeks Develop appropriate operating ranges. Develop and finalize operating, cleaning, and maintenance procedures. Demonstrate production and delivery of water of the required quality.
2 2-4 weeks Demonstrate consistent operation within established ranges. Demonstrate consistent production and delivery of water of the required quality.
3 One year Demonstrate extended performance. Ensure that potential seasonal variations are evaluated and treated.

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念：為何水系統確效需要「時間」

大多數設備確效可以在幾天到幾週內完成。但水系統的確效整個流程需要 超過一年，原因有三：

  • 微生物行為的時間依賴性：Biofilm 的建立與成熟需要數週到數月
  • 進水水質的季節變化：夏季微生物負荷高、冬季水溫低影響系統效率
  • 使用模式的變異：生產旺季/淡季的用水量差異會影響管路內水的滯留時間

所以，水系統確效不是「拍一張快照」，而是「錄一整年的影片」來證明系統在所有合理操作條件下都能穩定運作。

Phase 1
2-4 weeks
建立操作範圍
→
Phase 2
2-4 weeks
證明一致性
→
Phase 3
1 year
長期性能驗證

重點提示：Phase 期間水可以用於生產嗎？

原文明確指出：只要分析結果合格，各階段產生的水都可以用於生產。這對 CDMO 非常重要，因為新廠或新系統在確效期間不能生產 = 一整年沒有營收。

實務上的做法是：

  • Phase 1 完成後，如果所有結果合格，通常會發一份 interim report 來支持「有條件使用」
  • Phase 2 完成後發正式核准，允許常規生產使用
  • Phase 3 是在正常生產狀態下持續監控

關鍵是：你必須事先在 protocol 中定義「在什麼條件下允許使用」，而不是事後追認。

比喻說明 Analogy

想像你開了一家新餐廳。Phase 1 是試營運（soft opening）：測試菜單、調整流程、訓練團隊。Phase 2 是邀請制營業（limited opening）：證明你可以穩定出餐、品質一致。Phase 3 是正式營業的第一年：經歷過暑假旺季、冬天淡季、春節高峰，才能真正說「我們的品質是穩定的」。

10.3.1 Phase 1 — Intensive Monitoring 第一階段：密集監控

原文內容 Original Text

The purpose of this phase is to establish appropriate operating ranges and provide data for the development of cleaning and sanitization procedures and frequencies. Sampling should be performed after each step in the treatment process and from each point of use. In addition, the incoming feed water to the water system should be tested and verified to comply with the relevant "Drinking Water" regulations. The FDA Guide to Inspections of High Purity Water Systems suggests daily sampling for two to four weeks, but recognizes that other sampling programs may be acceptable.

In devising the sampling scheme, consideration should be given to the system configuration, maintenance cycles, how the water is drawn for use, and the expected or potential variation in chemistry and microbiological attributes, at each potential sample point. In treatment, chemistry testing is specific for each processing step and microbiological testing between each component is important to determine the microbial load and the component's ability to manage the load.

At the end of this phase, the SOPs for system operation and maintenance should be developed and approved for continued interim use during the next phase. System logs, documentation for critical parameters (e.g., conductivity and TOC data, sanitization data, etc.), and responses to critical alarms or action levels should be reviewed, to verify the appropriate procedures are in place. In addition, the process that will be followed to investigate a confirmed test failure should be developed at this time. The intent of this process is to assess whether a failure is localized (i.e., isolated to a specific port) or systematic, and to define how different types of failures will be handled.

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念：Phase 1 的三大產出

Phase 1 不只是「取樣」，它要產出三項關鍵成果：

  • 操作範圍（Operating Ranges）：基於實際數據而非設計假設的系統運行參數
  • SOP 初版：操作、清洗、消毒的標準作業程序，經過實際驗證
  • 偏差調查流程：如何區分「局部問題」和「系統性問題」的決策邏輯

重點提示：每個使用點都要取樣

原文強調 sampling from each point of use。這在大型水系統中意味著：

  • 一個典型的 CDMO WFI loop 可能有 20-40 個使用點
  • 每天每個點取樣 = 每天 20-40 個樣本
  • 持續 2-4 週 = 280-1,120 個樣本

這是非常大的工作量和成本。因此，取樣計畫的設計必須在「統計有效性」和「實務可行性」之間取得平衡。

CDMO 實務：Localized vs. Systematic Failure

原文要求在 Phase 1 結束前建立偏差調查流程，區分：

  • 局部問題（Localized）：例如某個特定使用點的閥門汙染 — 只需清洗該點並增加取樣頻率
  • 系統性問題（Systematic）：例如整個迴路的微生物計數都在上升 — 可能需要整個系統消毒，甚至暫停使用

CDMO 的痛點：客戶產品正在生產線上，如果判斷為系統性問題需要停機消毒，營收損失和客戶關係的衝擊都很大。所以這個調查流程的邏輯必須在 Phase 1 就建立好，不能等到問題發生才臨時決定。

練習思考 Practice Questions

  1. 原文說 FDA 建議每天取樣 2-4 週，但也承認「other sampling programs may be acceptable」。如果你要設計一個「替代方案」，你的科學理據（scientific rationale）會是什麼？
  2. Phase 1 結束時，如果某些取樣點的微生物結果偏高但仍在規格內，你會如何處理？直接進入 Phase 2 還是延長 Phase 1？
10.3.2 Phase 2 — Consistent Operation 第二階段：一致性驗證

原文內容 Original Text

The second phase is intended to demonstrate that the system consistently operates within pre-determined operating ranges and delivers water of the required quality when operated in accordance with the SOPs. The FDA Guide suggests that the sampling scheme and duration should be the same as for phase one. During phases 1 and 2, multiple samples should be taken from each point of use. Sampling methodology should be representative of the way water will be used. For example, sampling should not involve a lengthy preliminary purge if water usage will be direct and immediate. If water is used through an attached hose, then the sample should be taken from the hose.

It is recommended that each point be sampled at least once per week as a minimum. In this manner, localized contamination may be discovered. (Note that too frequent sampling of little used points may mask incipient localized microbial growth by artificial purging.) Phase 2 allows the gathering of sufficient data to establish microbial alert and action limits (see Section 10.4).

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念：Phase 2 的核心要求 — 「一致性」

Phase 1 證明了「系統可以產出合格的水」，Phase 2 要證明的是「系統每次都能產出合格的水」。這兩者的差異就像：

  • Phase 1：「這個投手可以投出 150km/h 的球」
  • Phase 2：「這個投手每場比賽都能穩定投出 150km/h 的球」

重點提示：取樣方法必須「代表實際使用方式」

這是一個常被忽略但極為重要的原則。原文舉了兩個例子：

  • 不要長時間預沖：如果實際使用時是「打開閥門直接用」，取樣時也應該直接取，不應該先沖 5 分鐘。否則你取到的是「沖洗後的好水」，不是「使用者實際拿到的水」。
  • 從軟管末端取樣：如果使用點接了軟管，取樣就要從軟管末端取，因為軟管本身可能是汙染源。

隱藏陷阱：過度取樣反而掩蓋問題

原文有一句非常重要但容易被忽略的警告：

"Too frequent sampling of little used points may mask incipient localized microbial growth by artificial purging."

意思是：如果某個使用點平常很少用（例如備用管路），頻繁取樣等於頻繁沖洗，反而把正在形成的 biofilm 沖掉了。取樣結果看起來很好，但實際上問題被掩蓋了。

CDMO 建議：對低使用率的 point of use，取樣頻率應該模擬實際使用頻率，而不是為了「數據好看」而天天取樣。

Alert Limit vs. Action Limit

Phase 2 的另一個關鍵產出是建立 Alert / Action Limit：

  • Alert Limit（警戒限）：趨勢偏離的早期警告，通常觸發增加取樣頻率和調查
  • Action Limit（行動限）：接近規格邊界，觸發糾正行動（消毒、維修等）

這些限值應基於 Phase 1 和 Phase 2 的實際數據統計建立，而非僅引用 USP 或行業慣例。

10.3.3 Phase 3 — Extended Performance 第三階段：長期性能驗證

原文內容 Original Text

The third phase is intended to demonstrate that, when operated for an extended time period (typically one year), the system produces and delivers water of the required quality, despite possible seasonal variations of the feed water. Sample locations, frequencies, and test requirements are based on the established procedures.

For WFI systems, the FDA Guide recommends sampling daily from a minimum of one point of use, with all points of use tested weekly. At the end of this phase (i.e., after a full year of testing), the validation is considered completed. In most cases, ongoing monitoring will establish a continuing record of water quality.

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念：一年 = 四季全覆蓋

Phase 3 的持續時間是一年，目的不是「多取一年的樣」，而是要確保系統經歷過所有可能的環境變化：

  • 春季：融雪導致進水水質劣化（有機物、微生物增加）
  • 夏季：高溫促進微生物生長，進水水溫升高影響 RO 效率
  • 秋季：落葉季節影響地表水質
  • 冬季：低溫影響系統效率，但微生物風險較低

台灣的情境也類似：梅雨季（進水濁度高）、颱風季（可能影響市水供應）、夏季高溫等都是必須涵蓋的變異情境。

重點提示：WFI 的取樣頻率要求

對 WFI 系統，FDA 的建議取樣頻率是：

  • 每日：至少從一個使用點取樣
  • 每週：所有使用點都要取到

注意這是 Phase 3（長期監控）的頻率，比 Phase 1/2 已經降低。但對 Purified Water（PW）系統，指南沒有這麼嚴格的要求 — 這反映了 WFI 對注射劑品質的關鍵影響。

比喻說明 Analogy

Phase 3 就像新車的「第一年保固期」。製造商讓你在各種路況、天氣、負載條件下開一年，如果一年下來車子都表現正常，才真正確認這台車的品質。同樣地，水系統要經過春夏秋冬的考驗，才能說「驗證完成」。

CDMO 實務：Phase 3 期間的商業運營

好消息是：Phase 3 通常在正常生產狀態下進行，不需要額外的停機時間。但需注意：

  • Phase 3 期間的偏差（OOS/OOT）仍需正式調查和處理
  • 如果 Phase 3 期間出現重大系統變更，可能需要重新評估是否「重啟」Phase 3
  • Phase 3 的最終報告是水系統完整驗證的關鍵文件，稽查員必看
10.4 Acceptance Criteria 接受標準

原文內容 Original Text

Acceptance criteria for water are dependent upon its use. For both Purified Water and WFI, the chemical acceptance criteria are clearly described in the US Pharmacopoeia (USP). It is expected that a well designed water system, operating within specified design parameters will consistently be able to meet these criteria. Therefore, failures in chemical analysis during phases 1 and 2 must be investigated, the reason for failure corrected, and (except where errors in sampling or laboratory error are clearly indicated) the sampling phase extended to re-establish consistency of performance.

Microbial quality is not specified by the USP, but is established by the user based upon water use. The USP does recommend action limits for the different waters in its General Information chapter. These are 10 CFU/100ml for WFI and 100 CFU/ml for Purified Water. These may be employed as initial acceptance criteria for system qualification, although some flexibility is allowable, depending upon system design and use.

It is permissible that a single excursion, followed by acceptable re-sampling would not constitute a failure. In addition, because of the inherently bacteriostatic nature of WFI production and distribution systems, it should be expected that the large majority of samples should be well below the initial acceptance criterion. Therefore, for WFI it is prudent to establish a sample average acceptance criterion, which will be below the limit for a single sample. Failure investigation would be handled similarly to chemical analysis failure.

During phases 1 and 2, normal system microbial limits may be established. Acceptance criteria may then be converted to alert and action limits for use during phase 3 and beyond. These would take into account repeated excursions from the norm as well as step increases in micro count.

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念：化學 vs. 微生物接受標準的差異

面向 化學（Chemical） 微生物（Microbial）
來源 USP 明確定義（Conductivity, TOC） USP 僅「建議」，由使用者自行設定
WFI 限值 Conductivity ≤1.3 μS/cm @25°C; TOC ≤500 ppb 10 CFU/100ml（USP 建議 Action Limit）
PW 限值 同上 100 CFU/ml（USP 建議 Action Limit）
失敗處理 必須調查、矯正、延長取樣期 單次偏移 + 重新取樣合格 = 可接受

重點提示：「樣本平均值」接受標準

原文提出一個非常實用的概念：對 WFI 系統，除了單一樣本的限值外，應建立 sample average acceptance criterion。

邏輯是：WFI 系統（通常 80°C 以上循環或使用蒸餾法）本質上是抑菌的。如果系統設計和運行正常，大多數樣本結果應該遠低於 10 CFU/100ml。如果平均值接近限值，即使每個單一樣本都「合格」，系統仍然有問題。

建議的做法：

  • Action Limit: 10 CFU/100ml（單一樣本）
  • Alert Limit: 1-3 CFU/100ml（基於實際數據的統計分析）
  • 平均值目標: <1 CFU/100ml（系統正常運行的預期表現）

重要觀點：化學失敗的嚴肅性

原文明確指出：化學分析失敗比微生物失敗更為嚴重。原因是：

  • 一個「設計良好」的水系統不應該出現化學不合格
  • 化學不合格代表系統設計或運行有根本問題
  • 必須找到根本原因、糾正、然後延長取樣期重新證明一致性

相比之下，微生物偶爾偏高可能是取樣操作、環境因素等造成，單次偏移加重取樣合格是可接受的。

比喻說明 Analogy

化學指標就像人的「血壓」— 一個設計良好的身體（系統），血壓應該一直正常。偶爾偏高一次可能是緊張，但如果持續偏高就是系統問題。微生物指標則像「感冒次數」— 即使很健康的人也可能偶爾感冒（偶發微生物偏移），但如果經常感冒就要查免疫系統（系統性問題）。

練習思考 Practice Questions

  1. 你的 WFI 系統在 Phase 1 期間，某個使用點連續兩天微生物結果為 5 CFU/100ml（低於 10 CFU/100ml 的限值但明顯高於其他點的 <1 CFU/100ml）。你會如何處理？
  2. 原文說化學失敗時需要「extended sampling phase」。如果 Phase 2 第三週出現一次 conductivity 失敗，後來確認是取樣瓶汙染，你是否仍需延長 Phase 2？為什麼？
  3. 你的客戶要求 PW 系統的微生物限值設為 10 CFU/ml（比 USP 建議的 100 CFU/ml 嚴格 10 倍）。你會如何評估這個要求的合理性？
10.5 Qualification Reports 確效報告

原文內容 Original Text

Qualification data should be compiled and conclusions written into a summary report. This is to be reviewed and approved by those responsible for operation and quality assurance of the water system.

An interim report should be written and approved at such time during the qualification sampling program, as it is desired to use water in production activities.

A summary report should be prepared at the conclusion of phase 2, periodic updates provided throughout phase 3 and a major update issued at the conclusion of phase 3.

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念：確效報告的時間線

水系統的確效報告不是「寫一份就好」，而是一系列的文件：

時機 報告類型 目的
Phase 1 結束 Interim Report 支持「有條件使用」的決定
Phase 2 結束 Summary Report 正式確認系統合格，允許常規生產使用
Phase 3 期間 Periodic Updates 持續追蹤，確認長期趨勢
Phase 3 結束 Major Update / Final Report 水系統完整驗證的最終文件

重點提示：Interim Report 的重要性

Interim report 是 CDMO 營運的關鍵文件。沒有它，你不能在確效期間使用水進行生產。撰寫時應包含：

  • Phase 1 所有數據的彙整與統計分析
  • 所有測試項目的合格/不合格判定
  • 任何偏差及其調查結論
  • 有條件使用的限制（如適用）
  • 後續 Phase 2/3 的取樣計畫確認

這份報告必須經過 QA 審核和核准。稽查員看到這份文件，才會認可你在確效期間的生產批次是合法的。

CDMO 實務建議

建議在 Phase 3 的 periodic updates 中加入趨勢分析圖表：

  • 微生物計數的時間趨勢圖（每個使用點分開繪製）
  • conductivity 和 TOC 的趨勢圖
  • 進水水質的季節變化對照
  • 消毒/維護事件的時間標記

這些圖表不僅是確效文件的組成部分，也是日後年度產品品質回顧（APR/PQR）中水系統部分的基礎數據。

10.6 Change Control and Requalification 變更管制與再確效

原文內容 Original Text

Changes to the system must be assessed with regard to potential impact of the change on the entire system. Required action would be determined based on that assessment. It may involve extensive re-qualification, localized increase in sampling frequency, or inclusion in the routine monitoring program.

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念：變更影響的三個等級

原文雖然只有短短一段，但包含了變更管制的核心框架。每個變更都應根據影響評估，對應到三種行動之一：

影響等級 變更範例 對應行動
高 更換 RO 膜型號、改變消毒方式、修改管路配置 Extensive re-qualification（可能需重跑 Phase 1+2）
中 更換某使用點閥門、增加新使用點、維修管路焊接 Localized increase in sampling frequency
低 更換同型號耗材（濾芯、墊圈）、調整消毒時間參數 Inclusion in routine monitoring program

重點提示：「整個系統」的影響評估

原文特別強調要評估變更對整個系統的影響，而不只是變更點本身。原因是水系統是連續循環的封閉系統：

  • 在上游更換了一個組件，可能改變整個迴路的水質
  • 增加一個使用點，可能改變整個系統的流速分布
  • 調整消毒頻率，影響的是整個管路的微生物控制

這與一般設備的變更管制不同 — 更換一台壓錠機的模具，只影響那台機器。但更換水系統的一段管路，可能影響所有使用該水的產品。

交叉參照：ISPE Vol.5 變更管制

ISPE Vol.5 C&Q Guide 的 Chapter 8（Change Management）提供了更完整的變更管制框架：

  • 變更分類：Like-for-Like / Minor / Major / Critical
  • 風險評估工具：影響評估矩陣（Impact Assessment Matrix）
  • 再確效深度：從「文件審查」到「完整再驗證」的光譜

建議在水系統的變更管制 SOP 中，直接引用 Vol.5 的框架，並針對水系統的特殊性（連續循環、微生物風險）增加具體的評估準則。

CDMO 特別注意：客戶溝通

CDMO 水系統的變更管制有一個額外維度：客戶通知。水系統的重大變更可能影響所有使用該系統的客戶產品，因此：

  • 品質協議（QTA）中應明確定義水系統變更的通知範圍
  • 高影響變更可能需要客戶核准才能執行
  • 變更後的再確效數據可能需要提供給客戶作為其產品變更的支持文件

練習思考 Practice Questions

  1. 你的 WFI 系統需要更換 RO 膜（從 A 品牌換成 B 品牌，規格相同但材質不同）。你會將此歸類為哪個影響等級？需要什麼程度的再確效？
  2. 生產團隊要求在 WFI loop 上增加一個新的使用點（用於新客戶的填充線）。除了管路的物理安裝，你需要考慮哪些確效和監控方面的事項？
  3. 你的進水（市水）供應商通知你，他們將在下個月更換消毒劑（從氯改為氯胺）。這對你的水系統有什麼潛在影響？你的變更管制流程應如何回應？
Chapter Summary 本章總結

Key Takeaways 重點回顧

  • Documentation: Water system qualification requires enhanced documentation including isometric diagrams, weld records, passivation records, and slope verification — beyond standard IQ/OQ/PQ packages.
  • Three-Phase Sampling: Phase 1 (2-4 weeks) establishes operating ranges; Phase 2 (2-4 weeks) demonstrates consistency; Phase 3 (1 year) confirms long-term performance across seasonal variations.
  • Acceptance Criteria: Chemical criteria from USP are absolute; microbial criteria (10 CFU/100ml WFI, 100 CFU/ml PW) are user-established based on USP recommendations, with alert/action limits derived from actual system data.
  • Qualification Reports: A series of reports — interim, summary, periodic updates, and final — are required throughout the multi-phase qualification program.
  • Change Control: Every change must be assessed for system-wide impact, with response ranging from full requalification to inclusion in routine monitoring.

導師總結 Instructor Summary

本章核心訊息

水系統確效的本質是「用時間和數據證明系統的可靠性」。它不是一次性的「通過/不通過」測試，而是一個持續超過一年的證據積累過程。

對 CDMO 而言，這意味著：

  • 新廠規劃：水系統確效的時程（>1年）必須納入新廠投產的整體時程規劃
  • 日常運營：Phase 3 完成後，持續監控就是你的「保險」— 用持續的數據證明系統一直在控制中
  • 客戶信任：一套完整的三階段確效文件 + 持續的趨勢監控數據，是向客戶和稽查員展現水系統控制能力的最有力證據

與其他 ISPE 指南的連結

  • ISPE Vol.5（C&Q Guide）：提供系統分類、風險評估、leveraging commissioning 的完整框架。水系統作為 Direct Impact System，適用最嚴格的確效要求。
  • ISPE Vol.4 前面章節：系統設計（Chapter 3-7）的決策直接影響確效策略 — 例如 hot loop vs. ambient loop 決定了消毒頻率和微生物控制策略。
  • FDA Guide to Inspections of High Purity Water Systems：本章多次引用的 FDA 指南，提供了更具體的稽查視角。

綜合練習 Comprehensive Exercise

假設你是一家 CDMO 的工程/品質主管，公司正在建置一套新的 WFI 系統，預計 6 個月後開始供水給新的無菌充填線。請完成以下任務：

  1. 制定一份三階段取樣計畫的時間表，標註每個階段的開始條件、取樣頻率、和退出標準
  2. 定義初步的化學和微生物接受標準，說明你的理據
  3. 列出確效報告的交付時程表，標註每份報告需要哪些人審核/核准
  4. 設計一個變更管制的決策矩陣：列出 5 種常見的水系統變更場景，分別定義影響等級和對應的再確效要求

ISPE Baseline® Guide Volume 4: Water and Steam Systems — Chapter 10: Commissioning and Qualification

SterileGMP Knowledge Hub — Bilingual Educational Reference

For training and educational purposes only. Not a substitute for the original ISPE publication.

A1-3

Chapter A1-3: Appendix: USP & Passivation

附錄：USP水質與鈍化 | p165-p185

ISPE Baseline® Guide Volume 4: Water and Steam Systems | p165-p185

Learning Objectives / 學習目標

  1. Understand the USP three-stage conductivity testing methodology and temperature-dependent limits / 理解 USP 三階段電導率測試方法及溫度依賴性限值
  2. Describe TOC measurement principles, oxidation methods, and CO2 detection techniques / 描述 TOC 量測原理、氧化方法及 CO2 偵測技術
  3. Explain the differences between EP and USP water quality requirements / 說明 EP 與 USP 水質要求之差異
  4. Describe the passivation process, chemical alternatives, and application methods / 描述鈍化製程、化學替代品及應用方法
  5. Identify tests for cleanliness and passivity verification / 辨識清潔度與鈍化驗證的檢測方法
  6. Understand the rouging phenomenon, its causes, and remediation strategies / 理解紅銹現象、成因及修復策略
  7. Outline documentation requirements for passivation activities / 概述鈍化活動的文件記錄要求

11.1 USP Regulated Water Quality / USP 法規水質

Original Text

11.1.1 USP Three-Stage Conductivity Levels

In order to set the maximum allowable conductivity, USP determined the limit concentration of the least conductive water attribute species used in USP 22 to be chloride at 0.47 ppm and 1.01 µS/cm at the standard temperature. When actual measurements of conductivity are evaluated at temperatures above the standard 25°C, the least conductive water attributes between pH 5 and 7, changes to favor ammonia at the maximum acceptable concentration of 0.3 ppm, using chloride as the electro-neutrality-balancing counter ion.

Stage 1 — In-Line Test

Primarily intended as an in-line test. Measure water conductivity and temperature using a non-compensated conductivity sensor using a suitable container or an in-line measurement.

Stage 2 — Off-Line Grab Sample

If the sample fails the Stage 1 test, adjust the temperature to 25°C and stir. If the sample stabilizes to the minimum conductivity value listed in the Stage 3 table, the water meets the requirements.

Stage 3 — Laboratory Test

Measure pH within 5 minutes of the Stage 2 conductivity reading after increasing its ionic strength to allow a pH reading, using saturated potassium chloride solution at 3%.

Commentary / 教學解說

三階段電導率測試架構

核心概念：為什麼要三階段？

USP 設計三階段測試是為了在效率與準確性之間取得平衡：

  • Stage 1（線上）：快速篩檢，90%以上的樣品在此階段通過
  • Stage 2（離線）：溫度標準化到 25°C，排除溫度影響
  • Stage 3（實驗室）：加入 pH 測量，考慮鹼度對電導率的影響

生活比喻

就像機場安檢的三道關卡：第一道是金屬探測門（快速通過），第二道是手持掃描器（進一步確認），第三道是開箱檢查（完整檢驗）。大多數旅客在第一道就通過了。

實務重點

  • Stage 1 使用非溫度補償的感測器 — 這是刻意設計，因為限值已經按溫度區間設定
  • Stage 2 失敗才需要做 Stage 3 — 不是每個樣品都需要做到底
  • Stage 3 的 pH 測量必須在電導率讀數後 5 分鐘內完成，避免 CO2 吸收影響

11.1.3 In-Line (Stage 1) Conductivity Limits

Temperature Range (°C) Max Conductivity (µS/cm)
0 to 4.90.6
5 to 9.90.8
10 to 14.90.9
15 to 19.91.0
20 to 24.91.1
25 to 29.91.3
30 to 34.91.4
35 to 39.91.5
40 to 44.91.7
45 to 49.91.8
50 to 54.91.9
55 to 59.92.1
60 to 64.92.2
65 to 69.92.4
70 to 74.92.5
75 to 94.92.7
95 to 99.92.9
1003.1

Based on the chloride-ammonia model at the least conductive water attributes between pH 5 and 7.

電導率限值表解讀

溫度與電導率的關係

水溫越高，離子活動度越大，電導率自然越高。USP 的限值隨溫度上升而放寬：

  • 0°C 時限值僅 0.6 µS/cm
  • 25°C 時限值為 1.3 µS/cm（標準參考點）
  • 100°C 時限值放寬到 3.1 µS/cm

常見陷阱

  • 熱水循環系統（80°C）的線上電導率 2.5 µS/cm 是合格的 — 不要用 25°C 的標準來判斷
  • 75-94.9°C 合併為一個區間（2.7 µS/cm），這是因為在此高溫範圍內變化較小
  • 實際操作中，良好系統的電導率通常遠低於限值

Stage 2 & 3 補充

Stage 2：將樣品調到 25°C 測量，限值為 2.1 µS/cm。

Stage 3：在 25°C 下測量 pH，再對照 pH-電導率對照表判定。此階段考慮了大氣 CO2 溶入的影響。

11.1.5-11.1.6 Total Organic Carbon (TOC) / 總有機碳

Original Text

11.1.5 TOC Requirements

TOC is an indirect measure, as carbon, of organic molecules present in high purity water. A TOC limit was determined by USP to be 0.5 ppm or 500 ppb, based on the results of studies and an industry wide survey of pharmaceutical water systems.

Instruments are available for measuring TOC in-line from slip streams and from grab samples. USP have not prevented acceptable technologies from being used, but limit the methods to instruments that are capable of:

  • Completely oxidizing the organic molecules to carbon dioxide (CO2)
  • Measuring the CO2 levels as carbon
  • Discriminating between Inorganic Carbon (IC) and CO2 from organic oxidation
  • Maintaining an equipment limit of detection of 0.05 mg/l or lower
  • Periodically demonstrating equipment "suitability"
Commentary / 教學解說

TOC 測量基礎

什麼是 TOC？

Total Organic Carbon（總有機碳）是水中有機物含量的間接指標。USP 限值為 500 ppb。

基本原理：將水中有機物完全氧化為 CO2，再量測 CO2 的濃度換算回碳含量。

TC = TOC + IC（Total Carbon = 有機碳 + 無機碳）

生活比喻

TOC 測量就像燃燒垃圾來秤重：你把所有有機物燒成 CO2，量測 CO2 的量，就知道原本有多少有機物。但要先扣除水中原本就有的 CO2（無機碳），才能得到真正的有機碳含量。

偵測極限的重要性

USP 要求儀器的偵測極限 ≤0.05 mg/L（50 ppb），這是限值 500 ppb 的 1/10。在製藥用水中，IC 通常很低，不需要特別去除。

11.1.6 Types of TOC Analyzers

Oxidation Methods

Method of Oxidation CO2 Detection Requires Chemicals/Gases
High temperature combustion (>500°C)NDIRYes
Heat activated persulfateNDIRYes
UV activated persulfateNDIR or CO2 selective conductivityYes
UV light only (<195 nm)Direct conductivity or CO2 selective conductivityNo

CO2 Detection Methods

Four CO2 detection methods are currently used:

  • NDIR (Non-Dispersive Infrared) — measures IR absorption of CO2 in gas phase; dynamic range ~1.5-2 orders of magnitude; LOD 2-10 ppb
  • CO2 Selective Conductivity — uses special membrane to diffuse CO2 into DI water collector; calibration stability 6-12 months; LOD 0.05 ppb
  • Direct Conductivity — measures conductivity of sample directly; requires water to be CO2/DI water equilibrium only; LOD ~0.05 ppb
  • Differential Conductivity — pre/post oxidation difference; partially oxidizes organics to organic acids stage

TOC 分析儀技術比較

四種氧化方法

  • 高溫燃燒（>500°C）：最暴力、最徹底，適合未知樣品，但需要壓縮氧氣
  • 熱活化過硫酸鹽：用熱能產生強氧化性自由基，可處理大分子
  • UV 活化過硫酸鹽：用紫外線激活，可量測 50-100 ppm 的高濃度 TOC
  • 純 UV 光（<195 nm）：不需外加化學品！最適合線上監測，但上限較低（~250 ppb 無氧，~2000 ppb 含氧）

選擇指南

  • 線上持續監測：純 UV 光法最簡便（不需化學品），適合製藥用水
  • 實驗室分析：高溫燃燒或過硫酸鹽法，量測範圍更廣
  • 過硫酸鹽法對 >30 µm 的大顆粒氧化困難

CO2 偵測方法比較

方法LOD校正穩定性特點
NDIR2-10 ppb需定期校正最常用於實驗室型
CO2 選擇性電導率0.05 ppb6-12 個月用薄膜分離，高選擇性
直接電導率~0.05 ppb6-12 個月水必須是純 DI + CO2
差分電導率--量測有機酸而非 CO2

11.1.7 Limit Response & System Suitability Testing

Apparatus Requirements:

  • Reagent Water (rW): complying with USP definition, TOC ≤ 0.25 mg/L
  • Standard Solution (rS): containing 1.19 mg of sucrose
  • System Suitability Solution (rSS): containing 1,4-Benzoquinone RS (0.5 mg/L carbon)

Instrument Standardization (Limit Response Test):
The test solution meets the requirement if:

rt < (rS − rW)

Instrument Suitability (Response Efficiency):
An instrument is "suitable" if its response efficiency is within ±15% of theoretical:

100 × [(rSS − rW) / (rS − rW)] = 100% ± 15%

For in-line testing, standardization once per operating day may be appropriate. Suitability tests may range from each sample test (off-line) to once per month (in-line).

限度回應與系統適用性測試

兩個關鍵公式

限度回應測試（Limit Response）：

r_t < (r_S - r_W)
測試樣品回應 < (蔗糖標準液回應 - 試劑水回應)

適用性測試（Suitability）：

100 x [(r_SS - r_W) / (r_S - r_W)] = 100% +/- 15%
苯醌回應效率必須在 85%-115% 之間

為什麼用蔗糖和苯醌？

蔗糖（sucrose）：非常容易被氧化，作為「最佳情況」的標準品。

1,4-苯醌（benzoquinone）：較難被氧化，用來測試儀器能否處理「困難」的有機物。如果儀器對苯醌的回應效率在 ±15% 以內，表示它能有效氧化各類有機物。

線上 vs 離線測試頻率

  • 線上儀器做標準化測試需要斷開水系統，成本高
  • 每日一次標準化可接受；適用性測試可每月一次
  • 有些 TOC 儀器內建自動化測試功能，可節省大量時間和費用

11.1.8 USP Microbial and Endotoxin Testing

Microbial and Endotoxins are traditionally sampled at the points of use in a water system.

11.1.9 USP pH Testing

pH testing to support in-line conductivity testing was eliminated in May 1998.

Calibration of pH instruments: See Chapter 2, Section 2.3 USP Regulated Water Quality.

Dual Purpose TOC/Conductivity Meters

A number of TOC meters designed for in-line applications include conductivity measurements. To use this dual purpose instrument, the conductivity cell must be separately calibrated with a NIST-traceable precision resistor accurate to ±0.1%.

微生物、內毒素與 pH 測試

微生物與內毒素取樣

必須在使用點（POU）取樣，因為微生物可能在管路末端繁殖。不能只在產水點取樣就代表整個系統合格。

pH 測試的演變

1998 年 5 月起，USP 取消了用 pH 測試來輔助線上電導率測試的要求。原因：

  • 高純水的 pH 極難準確測量
  • 三階段電導率測試已涵蓋 pH 的影響
  • EP 仍保留獨立的 pH 測試要求

雙用途 TOC/電導率儀器

越來越多線上 TOC 儀器兼具電導率測量功能。要符合 USP 要求，電導率池必須獨立校正，使用 NIST 可追溯的精密電阻器（精度 ±0.1%）。

11.2 European Perspective / 歐洲觀點

Original Text

11.2.1 Regulatory Structure

The pharmaceutical industry within the EU is regulated by the European Agency for the Evaluation of Medicinal Products (EMEA), based in London. EMEA has three major functions:

  • Centralized drug approval — granting marketing authorization across Europe; mandatory for biotech products, optional for others
  • Supervision — continued use of medicinal products, including site inspections and pharmacovigilance
  • Coordination — European and international harmonization (ICH framework)

Within Member States, each nation has a national agency (e.g., MCA in UK, BfArM in Germany). The Pharmaceutical Inspection Convention (PIC) has been superseded by the Pharmaceutical Inspection Cooperation Scheme (PIC/S).

11.2.2 Regulations Governing Water and Steam

The EC recognizes the European Pharmacopoeia (EP) as the source of compendial water standards. The EP does not regulate Clean Steam. In Europe, chemical quality of steam for sterilization is governed by European Standard EN 285. UK guidance on steam quality is found in HTM-2010 and HTM-2031.

Commentary / 教學解說

歐洲法規架構

歐洲藥品監管體系

歐洲的架構是中央（EMEA）+ 國家機構雙層制：

  • EMEA 負責跨歐洲的集中審批（生技產品強制）
  • 各國機構負責國內註冊和工廠檢查
  • PIC/S 提供非約束性的檢查指南，促進協調

水和蒸汽的法規來源

  • 水質標準：EP（歐洲藥典）
  • 潔淨蒸汽品質：EN 285 + HTM-2010/2031
  • EP 不規範潔淨蒸汽 — 這是歐洲與美國的重要差異
  • EU cGMP 附錄僅要求蒸汽品質「適當」且不含汙染物

11.2.3 Water Issues

  • While USP requires starting point must meet national drinking water standard, EP requires only that the history of the starting water be documented.
  • EP allows production of WFI by distillation only. Some pharmacopoeias allow WFI by reverse osmosis.
  • EP requires sample sizes of 100 ml for purified water and 500 ml for WFI. USP is not definitive on sample sizes.
  • EP has not accepted substitution of conductivity/resistivity for traditional wet chemistry analyses.
  • EP has not added the test for TOC.
  • EP continues to require the test for pH as an independent test.
  • European inspectors are sensitive to the drainability of systems through points of use.

11.2.4 Steam Issues

  • HTM-2010 emphasizes dryness requirements. EN-285 requires dryness value ≥ 0.9 (0.95 for metal loads).
  • Superheat concern: superheated steam is less effective for sterilization; may result from adiabatic expansion.
  • Non-Condensable Gases (NCGs): air and CO2 from failure to degas feed water; can create pockets preventing steam penetration.

EP vs USP 關鍵差異

水質要求差異總覽

項目USPEP
原水要求必須符合飲用水標準僅需記錄原水歷史
WFI 製法蒸餾或 RO 皆可僅限蒸餾
電導率測試三階段測試取代濕化學未接受電導率替代
TOC 測試已納入（500 ppb）未納入
pH 測試1998 年取消仍為獨立測試
取樣量未明確規定PW 100 mL / WFI 500 mL

蒸汽品質三大關注點

  1. 乾燥度（Dryness）：≥0.9（金屬負載 ≥0.95）。過濕會造成多孔負載潮濕；「過乾」可能過熱
  2. 過熱度（Superheat）：過熱蒸汽殺菌效果差。控制夾套溫度和閥門壓降可預防
  3. 不凝結氣體（NCGs）：空氣和 CO2 會形成「氣穴」，阻止蒸汽穿透。預處理進水加熱至 80°C 以上脫氣

實務影響

如果你的產品同時銷售歐美市場，水系統設計必須同時滿足 USP 和 EP 要求。最安全的做法是採用最嚴格的標準組合：USP 的電導率/TOC 測試 + EP 的蒸餾法 WFI + EP 的取樣量。

11.3 Passivation / 鈍化

Original Text

11.3.1 Introduction

Pharmaceutical equipment and high purity water systems are designed so that product contact surfaces are not reactive, additive, or absorptive. High purity water systems are primarily composed of austenitic stainless steel (SS) due to their corrosion resistant and contaminant free properties.

Passivation is performed to maximize the metal's corrosion resistance. The stainless steel is sulfuric/nitric/hydrofluoric acid pickled at the mill to remove manganese sulfide inclusions, scale, and other impurities. As the steel is removed from the pickling bath, a thin oxide layer forms immediately over the surface. This oxide layer is what renders the stainless steel passive and non-reactive to corrosion.

Any 300 series chromium steels containing 17% or more chromium that has been handled, welded, or worked should be passivated prior to service.

11.3.3 The Chemical Process

Establishing a passive surface or film on austenitic SS is essential to maximize corrosion resistance. Passive surfaces occur naturally when exposed to an oxidizing environment. Nitric acid solution (HNO3) is an oxidizing acid which erodes the metal, and this initial reaction resists further chemical reaction on the surface. The chromium oxide film thickness typically ranges from 0.5-5.0 nm, averaging 2.0-3.0 nm. The chrome to iron ratio should be at least 1:1, with ratios of 2:1 or more being optimal.

Commentary / 教學解說

鈍化基礎原理

什麼是鈍化？

鈍化是在不鏽鋼表面形成一層氧化鉻保護膜的過程。這層膜：

  • 厚度僅 0.5-5.0 nm（奈米級，肉眼不可見）
  • 平均 2-3 nm
  • 使金屬表面「鈍性」— 不再與環境發生化學反應

生活比喻

鈍化就像給不鏽鋼「打蠟」。新車出廠時有保護漆，但如果刮傷了（像焊接破壞表面），就需要重新上漆（鈍化）。這層「蠟」是氧化鉻，只有幾奈米厚，但能有效阻擋腐蝕。

關鍵數據

  • 鉻鐵比（Cr/Fe ratio）：至少 1:1，最佳 ≥2:1
  • 鉻含量 ≥17% 的 300 系列不鏽鋼都需要鈍化
  • 任何經過焊接、加工、搬運的表面都應鈍化
  • 焊接會破壞現有的被動膜，特別是熱影響區

11.3.4 Passivation Procedures

All passivation procedures consist of four main steps:

  1. Wash (Solvent Degreasing) — Remove dirt, dust, oil, and grease using a water-soluble detergent or solvent
  2. Water Rinse — Remove dissolved soils and detergent
  3. Acid Wash (Passivation Step) — Remove free iron, metallic residues, oxides, and corrosion products. Inorganic acids are typically used.
  4. Final Water Rinse — Flush until effluent quality equals influent quality

Reference: ASTM A 380-96, "Standard Recommended Practice for Cleaning and Descaling Stainless Steel Parts, Equipment and Systems."

Guidelines for establishing effective procedures:

  • Start with an accepted or specified procedure
  • Obtain weld coupons from the system for testing
  • Perform specified and alternate procedures for comparison
  • Confirm effectiveness with field and/or laboratory testing
  • Include procedure confirmation in validation documentation

鈍化程序四步驟

四步驟流程

Step 1: 脫脂清洗
↓
Step 2: 水沖洗
↓
Step 3: 酸洗（鈍化）
↓
Step 4: 最終水沖洗

驗證重點

  • 使用系統的焊接試片來測試程序效果 — 不要只靠理論
  • 最終沖洗的判定標準：出水品質 = 進水品質
  • 所有程序開發資料應納入驗證文件包
  • 參考 ASTM A 380-96 是產業標準做法

11.3.5 Passivation Chemical Alternatives

Nitric acid (HNO3) is the most common acid specified for passivation. Reference: Federal Specification QQ-P-35C (1988).

The trend is to reduce chemical aggressiveness and consider safety, cost, and environmental impact.

Citric acid and ammonium citrate are gaining popularity as alternatives. ASTM A 380 refers to these as cleaning acids, not passivating acids (they are not oxidizers). To achieve true oxidation, chelating agents in conjunction with citric acid have been introduced.

Phosphoric acid is a weak oxidizing acid sometimes specified, but with no formal documentation as a passivating acid.

Chelants (sequestering agents) such as STPP, NTA, and EDTA may be compounded into acid passivation solutions to enhance metal ion extraction.

Orbital welding with electropolished tubing decreases the aggressiveness required of passivating acids during initial passivation.

鈍化化學品選擇

三大化學品比較

化學品類型優點缺點
硝酸（HNO3）強氧化酸最有效、產業標準腐蝕性強、環境衝擊大
檸檬酸清洗酸（非氧化）安全、環保本身不氧化，需加螯合劑
磷酸弱氧化酸溫和無正式文件支持

產業趨勢

從硝酸轉向檸檬酸 + 螯合劑的組合：

  • 安全性：檸檬酸對人員和環境更安全
  • 成本：廢液處理更簡單便宜
  • 有效性：配合 EDTA 等螯合劑可達到類似效果
  • 電拋光管材 + 軌道焊接 = 可使用更溫和的鈍化條件

ASTM 的區分

ASTM A 380 將檸檬酸歸類為清洗酸（cleaning acid）而非鈍化酸（passivating acid），因為檸檬酸本身不是氧化劑。要實現真正的氧化鈍化，需要額外加入螯合劑或氧化劑。

11.3.6 Chemical Application Methods

MethodApplication
CirculationRecirculating through distribution systems (preferred); flow rate ≥5 ft/s (1.5 m/s)
One Way Intermittent FlowLarge non-recirculating, long one-way pipe runs
SprayingTank interiors
Tank ImmersionNumerous small parts, prefabricated tubing
Swabbing/WipingIsolated areas, tank/equipment exteriors

11.3.7 Tests for Cleanliness and Passivity

Cleanliness tests: Water break free surface test, wipe test, UV light testing (per ASTM A 380).

Indirect passivity tests (free iron detection):

  • Ferroxyl Test — test solution applied; blue stain within 15 seconds indicates free iron
  • Water Immersion/Wetting and Drying Test
  • High Humidity Test
  • Copper Sulfate Test
  • Ferroxyl/Potassium Ferricyanide-Nitric Acid Test

Direct passivity tests:

  • XPS (X-ray Photoelectron Spectroscopy) — measures oxidation states of surface elements
  • AES (Auger Electron Spectroscopy) — measures elemental Cr/Fe ratio with depth profiling (destructive)

應用方法與測試

應用方法選擇指南

  • 循環法（首選）：適合管路系統，流速 ≥1.5 m/s
  • 注意：高流速不等於顆粒去除！1000 英尺的管路需要約 25 小時的過濾循環才能完全去除顆粒
  • 噴塗法：適合槽體內部
  • 浸泡法：適合小零件和預製管件
  • 擦拭法：最後手段，用於無法噴塗或循環的區域

鈍化驗證測試分層

層級方法成本資訊量
清潔度水膜破裂、擦拭、UV低基本
間接鈍化Ferroxyl（藍色=有游離鐵）低中等
直接鈍化XPS / AES高完整

實務建議

  • Ferroxyl 測試最常用：便宜、快速（15 秒出結果）
  • XPS/AES 較昂貴，最適合用於焊接試片以驗證程序有效性
  • 假設邏輯：無游離鐵 → 表面夠乾淨 → 氧化膜可均勻形成

11.3.13 Rouging

Rouging is seen in many water systems, usually high temperature (80°C) distilled water and clean steam systems. Rouge is not limited to storage and distribution; it also can be found in distillation and clean steam generating equipment.

The main constituent of the rouge film is ferric oxide, but it can contain iron, chromium, and nickel of different forms. From AES analysis, the outer layer is carbon rich, and the underlying region is iron and oxygen rich.

Rouge can be observed in a variety of colors: orange, light-red, red, reddish-brown, purple, blue, gray, and black. It can range from a loose dust-like film to a tight pertinacious film requiring scraping.

"Traditionally the red rouges are most common in high purity high temperature water systems, while the blue/black rouges are typically found in clean steam systems."

Key places to look: still and clean steam generator discharge lines, tank water/vapor interface, pump heads, Teflon® diaphragms (rouge has affinity for Teflon®), and heat affected area of welds.

The presence of rouge has not been proven to affect water quality. The FDA has no written position addressing rouging. Their criterion remains meeting established USP standards.

Removal: phosphoric, citric, oxalic acids, and ammonium citrates depending on severity. Oxalic acid for worst cases. Passivation with nitric acid required after oxalic acid rinse.

紅銹（Rouging）現象

什麼是紅銹？

紅銹是在不鏽鋼水系統中自然形成的氧化鐵薄膜。主要成分是 Fe2O3（三氧化二鐵），也含鉻和鎳。

常見於：

  • 80°C 高溫純水系統
  • 潔淨蒸汽系統
  • 蒸餾設備

顏色代表什麼？

  • 紅色系（橙、紅、紅棕）：高溫純水系統最常見
  • 藍黑色系（紫、藍、灰、黑）：潔淨蒸汽系統常見
  • 可以是鬆散灰塵狀（容易擦掉）到頑固薄膜（需要刮除）
  • 紅銹有時是多層的，不同層有不同顏色和質地

FDA 的立場

FDA 沒有針對紅銹發布任何書面立場。唯一的標準是水質是否符合 USP 要求。紅銹本身未被證明會影響水質。

但業界的擔憂是：隨時間推移，薄膜可能脫落並分散到系統中 — 這在過濾使用點的濾器變色中可以觀察到。

去銹（Derouging）策略

嚴重程度化學品備註
輕微檸檬酸 / 磷酸溫和處理
中等檸檬酸銨-
嚴重草酸（Oxalic acid）之後必須用硝酸重新鈍化

案例：某冷 WFI 系統去銹鈍化三次，每次一週內又完全紅銹。原因始終未確定 — 紅銹具有高度場址特異性。

11.3.14 Preparing Systems for Passivation

  • Hydrostatic pressure testing first — all new or modified systems
  • Confirm compatibility of all components with passivating solutions (instrumentation, flow meters, valves, UV lights, pumps, seals, filter membranes, gaskets)
  • Remove incompatible items and replace with blanks, valves, or temporary jumper hoses
  • Isolate the system from existing systems, process equipment, utility tie-ins
  • Eliminate all dead legs — critical for chemical contact and complete rinsing
  • Ensure high point vents and low point drains for complete filling and draining
  • Review automated controls: valve alignments, temperature sensors, diverter valves

11.3.11 System Chemistry (Summary)

ChemistryDescription
1Nitric acid: ambient temp 30-60 min; 50-60°C for 20-40 min
2Alkaline degreasing: detergents + pH buffers + surfactants (1-2% / 0.2-0.5% / 0.01-0.2%)
3Citric acid/chelant: 3-5% citric acid + chelants + reducing agents
4Mineral acid (phosphoric, sulfuric, sulfamic): 3-10% at various temps
5Intensified acid/chelant: for high-temp oxide, silica, carbon films
6Sodium Hydrosulfite: 5% by weight, 120-160°F, 2-4 hours

11.3.15-11.3.16 Disposal & Documentation

Waste solutions can be neutralized; the primary concern is heavy metals (chromium, nickel) in the acid wash effluent. Three disposal options: chemical drains, contractor neutralization, or off-site disposal.

Documentation must include: passivation procedure, development data, testing procedures, calibration records, passivation log sheets, chemical batch records, and marked-up system drawings.

系統準備、化學配方與文件

鈍化前準備清單

  1. 完成水壓測試 — 確保系統無洩漏
  2. 確認所有組件與鈍化液相容性 — 不相容的移除
  3. 將系統與其他系統隔離
  4. 消除所有死角（dead legs）
  5. 確保高點排氣和低點排水
  6. 檢查自動控制 — 避免異常溫度觸發轉向閥

六種化學配方速查

  • C1 硝酸：標準鈍化，室溫 30-60 分鐘
  • C2 鹼性脫脂：去除有機膜和顆粒
  • C3 檸檬酸/螯合劑：可去除鐵、錳、鋁、銅等金屬
  • C4 礦酸：磷酸/硫酸/氨基磺酸，3-10%
  • C5 強化酸/螯合劑：對付高溫氧化膜和矽垢
  • C6 連二亞硫酸鈉：強還原劑，用於去銹

廢液處理三選項

  1. 排入化學排水系統（需有相容處理設施）
  2. 在承包商設備中中和後排放
  3. 場外處置（最貴，需驗證運輸商和處理廠資質）

關鍵：廢液中的鉻和鎳是優先污染物金屬，不可直接排入雨水排水系統。

文件記錄要求

鈍化文件應納入最終驗證文件包，包含：

  • 鈍化程序書
  • 程序開發資料（試片測試結果）
  • 測試程序與設備
  • 校正文件
  • 鈍化日誌表
  • 化學品批次記錄
  • 標記的系統圖紙（point check list）

Practice Questions / 練習思考

  1. Your hot WFI system at 80°C shows an in-line conductivity of 2.6 µS/cm. Does it pass Stage 1? What is the limit at this temperature? / 你的熱 WFI 系統在 80°C 下線上電導率為 2.6 µS/cm，是否通過 Stage 1？此溫度的限值是多少？
  2. An auditor asks why you are not performing pH testing on your PW system. How do you respond? / 稽核員問你為什麼 PW 系統不做 pH 測試，你如何回答？
  3. Your system serves both US and EU markets. Which WFI production method must you use? / 你的系統同時供應美國和歐洲市場，WFI 必須用什麼方法生產？
  4. After passivation, the Ferroxyl test shows a blue stain at one weld location. What is your next step? / 鈍化後，Ferroxyl 測試在一個焊接處出現藍色斑點，下一步該怎麼做？
  5. Your facility discovers rouging in a 6-month-old hot WFI system. The water still meets USP specs. What do you recommend? / 你的設施在一個使用 6 個月的熱 WFI 系統中發現紅銹，水質仍符合 USP 標準。你的建議是什麼？

Section Summary / 本節摘要

  • USP Three-Stage Conductivity: A tiered testing approach (in-line → off-line → lab) with temperature-dependent limits from 0.6 to 3.1 µS/cm / USP 三階段電導率：分層測試架構，溫度依賴限值 0.6-3.1 µS/cm
  • TOC Testing: Limit 500 ppb; four oxidation methods and four CO2 detection methods; suitability testing with sucrose and benzoquinone / TOC 測試：限值 500 ppb；四種氧化法與四種 CO2 偵測法
  • EP vs USP Differences: EP requires distillation-only WFI, retains pH testing, has not adopted conductivity or TOC testing / EP 與 USP 差異：EP 要求蒸餾法 WFI，保留 pH 測試，未採用電導率或 TOC 測試
  • Passivation: Four-step process (degrease, rinse, acid wash, rinse) to form protective chromium oxide film (0.5-5 nm); Cr/Fe ratio ≥1:1 / 鈍化四步驟建立氧化鉻保護膜
  • Chemical Alternatives: Industry trending from nitric acid to citric acid + chelants for safety and environmental reasons / 化學品趨勢從硝酸轉向檸檬酸 + 螯合劑
  • Rouging: Common but unregulated phenomenon; ferric oxide film in hot water/steam systems; site-specific; does not necessarily affect water quality / 紅銹：常見但無法規要求，場址特異性高，不一定影響水質
  • Documentation: Comprehensive records (procedures, logs, batch records, system drawings) required for validation / 文件記錄：完整的鈍化記錄需納入驗證文件

ISPE Baseline® Guide Volume 4: Water and Steam Systems

Section 10: Appendix - USP Water Quality, European Perspective & Passivation | p165-p185

SterileGMP Knowledge Hub — Bilingual Educational Reference

A4-6

Chapter A4-6: Appendix: Treatment Details

附錄：處理技術詳論 | p186-p210

ISPE Baseline® Guide Volume 4: Water and Steam Systems | p186-p205

本節學習目標

  • 理解水系統前處理（Pretreatment）四大類別：濁度/微粒去除、硬度/金屬離子去除、有機物/微生物控制、pH 與 CO2 管理
  • 掌握各前處理技術的原理、優缺點與選型邏輯：包括介質過濾、阻隔過濾、軟化、脫氣、奈米過濾、活性碳、UV 殺菌與氯處理
  • 了解鐵（Iron）、矽（Silica）、鋁（Aluminum）三種「問題離子」的特殊去除挑戰
  • 理解 pH 對碳酸鹽/重碳酸鹽/CO2 平衡的影響，以及對下游 RO 與離子交換系統設計的關鍵意義
  • 掌握最終處理（Final Treatment）中離子交換與逆滲透的設計參數、組件配置、操作限制與效能指標
  • 能比較離子交換（IX）與逆滲透（RO）在製藥用水系統中的適用場景與優缺點

Section Navigation 本節導覽

11.4.1 Barrier Filtration 11.4.2 Hardness & Ion Exchange 11.4.3 Acidification & Nanofiltration 11.4.4 Organics & Microbiology 11.4.4.4 Microbial Growth Control 11.4.5-11.4.6 pH & CO2 11.5.1 Ion Exchange for PW 11.5.2 RO for High Purity
11.4.1 Barrier Filtration Details（阻隔過濾詳述）

Original Text

Barrier filtration includes cartridge filtration, pre-coat filtration, ultrafiltration, and nanofiltration. This type of filtration has a "barrier" through which the water must flow. The barrier retains particulates that are removed by changing the barrier (cartridge and pre-coat) or by a purge stream (ultra and nanofiltration).

Barrier filtration is typically not used as the primary method for removal of particulates because of cost of the barrier, labor, and the frequent need for replacement due to the relatively high particulate loading in the water entering pretreatment. It is frequently used as a "final clean-up" after other pretreatment processes such as ion exchange or carbon bed filtration, and before going to a final treatment step such as reverse osmosis.

As a "final clean-up," the barrier is often cartridges with a nominal particle size retention of 1-10 μm for removal of particulate carry-over. If the objective is to remove insoluble forms of silica and iron and achieve a SDI (silt density index) of less than 5 for feed to an RO system, the absolute particle size retention might be <1 μm -- at the lower end of microfiltration or upper end of ultrafiltration.

Ultrafiltration (0.1-0.001 μm) and nanofiltration (0.005-0.0005 μm) are methods of barrier filtration. They are not used for primary removal of particulates due to cost and rapid blinding of the membrane.

Advantages: Low initial cost; can vary particle size retention by cartridge selection.

Disadvantages: Cartridge filtration is expensive for high solids concentration; potential for microbial growth in a non-chlorinated environment.

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念：阻隔過濾的定位

阻隔過濾在前處理中扮演的是「收尾清潔」角色，而非「主力去除」。原因很簡單：進水微粒量大，頻繁更換濾芯的成本太高。

典型配置是放在軟化器或活性碳之後、RO 之前，保護下游膜系統不被顆粒堵塞。

重點提示：SDI < 5 的意義

SDI（Silt Density Index，淤泥密度指數）是 RO 進水品質的關鍵指標。SDI < 5 是大多數 RO 膜製造商的最低要求，SDI < 3 為理想值。

如果水中有不溶性矽或鐵，可能需要用絕對過濾精度 <1 μm 的濾芯或超過濾（UF）來達到此目標。

比喻說明：過濾如同門禁系統

把水系統想成一棟大樓：
介質過濾（Media Filtration）= 大門口的保全，擋掉大部分訪客（粗顆粒）
阻隔過濾（Barrier Filtration）= 電梯前的門禁卡，只讓通過安檢的人進入（細顆粒把關）
RO 膜= 總裁辦公室的指紋鎖，最高等級過濾

每一層都有成本，所以先用便宜的保全擋掉大部分，再用昂貴的門禁做精細把關。

過濾類型孔徑範圍去除對象
Cartridge Filtration1-10 μm (nominal)顆粒結轉物
Microfiltration0.1-100 μm細菌、梨形鞭毛蟲
Ultrafiltration0.001-0.1 μm病毒、熱原、膠體
Nanofiltration0.0005-0.005 μm二價離子、有機物
11.4.2 Hardness and Metals — Ion Exchange（硬度與金屬離子交換）

Original Text

11.4.2.1 Water Softening

An ion exchange system consists of a tank containing small beads of synthetic resin treated to selectively absorb either cations or anions and exchange these ions based upon their relative activity. The process will continue until all available exchange sites are filled, at which point the resin is exhausted and must be regenerated.

The presence of calcium (Ca) and magnesium (Mg) in a water supply is commonly known as "hardness," usually expressed in grains per gallon (gpg). Ion exchange is the principal method of removing hardness. This is required to prevent scale formation in final treatment operations such as RO and distillation.

A standard water softener has four major components: a resin tank, resin, a brine tank, and a valve or controller. The resin beads initially absorb sodium ions during brine regeneration. The resin has a greater affinity for multi-valence ions such as calcium and magnesium than for sodium. As hard water passes through the resin, Ca, Mg, and other multivalent ions adhere to the resin, releasing sodium ions until equilibrium is reached.

Regeneration is achieved by passing a sodium chloride (NaCl) solution through the resin, exchanging the hardness ions for sodium ions. The resin's affinity for hardness ions is overcome by using a highly concentrated brine solution.

Advantages: Industry standard, low cost and effective. Works well in a chlorinated environment. Inherent flushing of some microbial growth during regeneration.

Disadvantages: Salt handling for brine regeneration and disposal of spent brine solution.

11.4.2.2 Demineralization/De-ionization

Three multivalent ions present unusual removal problems:

a) Iron — May change valence states from water-soluble ferrous to insoluble ferric state. Organisms such as Crenothrix, Sphaerotilus, and Gallionella use iron as an energy source, forming a rusty, gelatinous sludge that plugs piping and membranes.

b) Silica — May be soluble ionized species or insoluble material, sometimes as colloidal mixture. Insoluble silica removed by barrier filtration <0.5 μm (UF) or by nanofiltration/RO.

c) Aluminum — Can exist in multiple valences; its solubility is a function of pH. Between pH 6-8, hydrated aluminum oxides are only partially (~80%) rejected by RO, often leading to fouling.

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念：軟化的原理與必要性

軟化（Softening）是前處理中最基礎的步驟之一。目的很明確：去除鈣和鎂，防止下游 RO 膜或蒸餾器結垢（scaling）。

原理是離子交換：樹脂上的鈉離子（Na+）與水中的鈣（Ca2+）和鎂（Mg2+）交換。因為樹脂對二價離子的親和力大於單價離子，所以交換自然發生。

當所有交換位點都被佔滿，就需要用高濃度鹽水（brine）再生，把鈣鎂沖下來、重新裝上鈉離子。

比喻說明：軟化器如同停車場

想像樹脂是一個停車場，每個車位上停著一台小車（Na+）。大型車輛（Ca2+, Mg2+）進來時，因為體積大（多價），會自動擠走小車佔據車位。

當車位全被大車佔滿（樹脂耗竭），管理員就用一大批小車（高濃度 NaCl）湧入，用數量優勢把大車逼走。這就是「再生」。

重點提示：三大「問題離子」

  • 鐵（Fe）：會變價！溶解態（Fe2+）遇氧氣變成不溶態（Fe3+）沉澱，堵塞膜系統。某些細菌還會以鐵為能源，產生鐵鏽狀膠體。
  • 矽（Si）：同時有離子態和膠體態。離子態軟化器可減少，膠體態需要 UF（<0.5 μm）或 NF/RO 去除。
  • 鋁（Al）：pH 6-8 之間最棘手，RO 只能去除約 80%。如果鋁是主要問題，可能需要「軟化 + pH 調整 + RO」的三步驟組合。

練習思考 Practice Questions

  1. 為什麼軟化器要放在 RO 之前而不是之後？如果不做軟化直接進 RO 會發生什麼？
  2. 你的水源分析報告顯示鐵含量偏高且 pH 為 7.5，你會建議哪種前處理組合？為什麼？
11.4.3 Hardness and Metals — Other Processes（其他硬度去除程序）

Original Text

11.4.3.1 Acidification and Degasification

Use of a pH adjustment step is common to favor or inhibit the formation of CO2. Injecting acid to a pH of approximately 5.5 will maximize the concentration of CO2, whereas addition of a base to pH 8.3 will minimize CO2, converting it to carbonate ion.

If high levels of CO2 are present, it can be removed down to about 5-10 ppm with an atmospheric degasifier. An atmospheric degasifier has the potential of increasing bacterial burden and should be located where bacterial control measures are available.

The incoming water is adjusted to a pH in the range of 3.8-4.2 with sulfuric acid. The acidified water is sent to a packed column degasifier for removal of free CO2 by air. Removal efficiency is better than 98%. The pH after removal of CO2 will be in the range of 6.5-7.0. Just prior to feeding the RO unit, the pH should be adjusted to approximately 8.0-8.5.

Advantages: Replaces softener and handling of large amounts of salt; CO2 released to atmosphere; the added sulfate ion from acidification is easier to remove in RO than added sodium ion from softening.

Disadvantages: Handling acid for acidification; instrumentation and chemical handling for two pH adjustments.

11.4.3.2 Nanofiltration

Nanofiltration is a pressure driven membrane process with performance characteristics between RO and ultrafiltration. The theoretical pore size is one nanometer (10-9 meter). These membranes are sometimes referred to as "softening membranes".

The nanofiltration membrane offers high rejection of salts of divalent anions as well as organics with molecular weights above 200. Operating at 70-150 psig (4.76-10.2 bar) vs. 200-350 psig for RO.

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念：酸化脫氣法的邏輯

這個程序的核心思路是：用酸把碳酸鹽/重碳酸鹽全部轉化為 CO2 氣體，然後用脫氣塔吹掉。

步驟：加酸（pH 降到 3.8-4.2）→ 脫氣塔去除 CO2（>98%）→ 加鹼（pH 升到 8.0-8.5）→ 進 RO

這個方法可以取代軟化器，特別適合大流量（>50 gpm）或高硬度（>50 ppm）的場景。

重點提示：脫氣塔的微生物風險

脫氣塔會引入空氣，空氣中的細菌會進入水中。解決方案：

  • 在進氣管加裝 HEPA 過濾器
  • 將脫氣塔設置在有微生物控制措施的位置（例如兩段式 RO 之間）
  • 脫氣後的水通常收集在儲水槽中，可在此進行 TOC 去除和微生物控制

實務應用：奈米過濾 vs. 軟化器 vs. 酸化脫氣

方法適用場景能耗特點
軟化器通用，最常見低需要鹽水再生
酸化脫氣大流量 / 高硬度中需二次 pH 調整
奈米過濾中等水質需求低於 RO70-150 psig，同時去除有機物

奈米過濾典型去除率

溶質化學式分子量去除率 %
氯化鈉NaCl5860
碳酸氫鈣Ca(HCO3)216280
硫酸鎂MgSO412098
葡萄糖C6H12O618098
蔗糖C12H22O1134299

規律：分子量越大、價數越高，去除率越高。單價離子如 NaCl 只有 60%。

11.4.4 Organics and Microbiological Impurities（有機物與微生物雜質）

Original Text

11.4.4.1 Introduction

Organic and microbiological contaminants need to be addressed in water treatment systems. The concerns are twofold: contaminants entering the system and contaminants created/growing in the system.

The first issue is water source: well water typically has low organic loading; surface water (lake, river, reservoir) will probably contain relatively high organics with seasonal variation. Municipal water is usually chlorinated, keeping microbiological content low until chlorine/chloramine is removed.

11.4.4.2 Organic Contaminants

a) Bacterial Contamination — Usually expressed as "total viable microbial counts per ml" or Colony Forming Units (CFU) per unit volume.

b) Pyrogenic Contamination — Pyrogens are substances that can produce fever in mammals. Often endotoxins (lipopolysaccharides) shed by bacterial cells. Molecular weight generally 12,000 to 320,000. Quantified in Endotoxin Units (EU) per milliliter. Chemically and physically stable — not necessarily destroyed by conditions that kill bacteria.

c) Total Organic Carbon (TOC) — A measure of organic materials in mg/l or μg/l. A direct measure of oxidizable organic material. Not a good measure of microbial contamination.
